Seals the admin session ID using XChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD so that:\n4\t//! - The session ID is encrypted (confidentiality).\n5\t//! - The ciphertext is authenticated (integrity — any tampering is detected).\n6\t//!\n7\t//! Cookie wire format (base64 of):\n8\t//!      key,\n81\t            generated: true,\n82\t        }\n83\t    }\n84\t\n85\t    /// Load from environment variable, falling back to random generation.\n86\t    pub fn from_env_or_generate() ->         _ => Self::generate_random(),\n101\t        }\n102\t    }\n103\t\n104\t    /// Whether the key was generated at startup rather than loaded from env.\n105\t    pub fn is_generated(&self) -> self.key.ct_eq(&other.key).into()\n112\t    }\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t/// Sealed cookie value — the opaque blob stored in the cookie.\n116\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n117\tpub struct seal(session_id: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<Self, SealError> {\n125\t        let cipher = XChaCha20Poly1305::new_from_slice(&key.key)\n126\t            .map_err(|_| SealError::KeyError)?;\n127\t\n128\t        let mut nonce_bytes = [0u8; NONCE_LEN];\n129\t        rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut nonce_bytes);\n130\t        let nonce = XNonce::from_slice(&nonce_bytes);\n131\t\n132\t        let ciphertext = cipher\n133\t            .encrypt(nonce, session_id.as_bytes())\n134\t            .map_err(|_| SealError::EncryptError)?;\n135\t\n136\t        Ok(Self {\n137\t            nonce: nonce_bytes,\n138\t            ciphertext,\n139\t        })\n140\t    }\n141\t\n142\t    /// Unseal a cookie value,  .map_err(|_| SealError::KeyError)?;\n146\t\n147\t        let nonce = XNonce::from_slice(&self.nonce);\n148\t        let plaintext = cipher\n149\t            .decrypt(nonce, buf.extend_from_slice(&self.nonce);\n159\t        buf.extend_from_slice(&self.ciphertext);\n160\t        URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(&buf)\n161\t    }\n162\t\n163\t    /// Decode from base64 cookie SealError::MalformedCookie)?;\n168\t\n169\t        // Minimum size: nonce + 1 byte plaintext + tag\n170\t        if bytes.len() < NONCE_LEN + 1 + TAG_LEN {\n171\t            return Err(SealError::MalformedCookie);\n172\t        }\n173\t\n174\t        let mut nonce = [0u8; NONCE_LEN];\n175\t        nonce.copy_from_slice(&bytes[..NONCE_LEN]);\n176\t        let ciphertext = bytes[NONCE_LEN..].to_vec();\n177\t\n178\t        Ok(Self { nonce, ciphertext })\n179\t    }\n180\t}\n181\t\n182\t/// Seal a session ID into a base64 cookie value.\n183\tpub fn seal_session(session_id: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<String, SealError> {\n184\t    let sealed = SealedCookie::seal(session_id, key)?;\n185\t    Ok(sealed.encode())\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t/// Unseal a base64 cookie value into a session ID.\n189\tpub fn unseal_session(cookie_value: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<String, SealError> {\n190\t    let sealed = SealedCookie::decode(cookie_value)?;\n191\t    sealed.unseal(key)\n192\t}\n193\t\n194\t/// Errors during seal/unseal operations.\n195\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n196\tpub enum SealError {\n197\t    /// The seal key is invalid.\n198\t    KeyError,\n199\t    /// Encryption failed.\n200\t    EncryptError,\n201\t    /// Decryption failed — wrong key or tampered ciphertext.\n202\t    DecryptError,\n203\t    /// The cookie value is malformed (wrong length, bad base64).\n204\t    MalformedCookie,\n205\t    /// The decrypted plaintext is not valid UTF-8.\n206\t    InvalidUtf8,\n207\t}\n208\t\n209\timpl std::fmt::Display for SealError {\n210\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n211\t        match self {\n212\t            SealError::KeyError => write!(f, \"invalid seal key\"),\n213\t            SealError::EncryptError => write!(f, \"encryption failed\"),\n214\t            SealError::DecryptError => write!(f, \"decryption failed — wrong key or tampered cookie\"),\n215\t            SealError::MalformedCookie => write!(f, \"malformed cookie value\"),\n216\t            SealError::InvalidUtf8 => write!(f, \"decrypted value is not valid UTF-8\"),\n217\t        }\n218\t    }\n219\t}\n220\t\n221\timpl std::error::Error for SealError {}\n222\t\n223\t#[cfg(test)]\n224\tmod tests {\n225\t    use     let sealed1 = seal_session(session_id, &key).unwrap();\n249\t        let sealed2 = seal_session(session_id, &key).unwrap();\n250\t        // Different nonces produce different ciphertexts\n251\t        assert_ne!(sealed1, sealed2);\n252\t    }\n253\t\n254\t    #[test]\n255\t    fn wrong_key_fails() {\n256\t        let key1 = test_key();\n257\t        let key2 = different_key();\n258\t {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_a5dc7646e332401dbd92c957","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Scoped Meilisearch key rotation (plan §13.21).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Implements the leader-based rotation sequence:\n4\t//! 1. Mint new scoped key via Meilisearch `POST /keys`\n5\t//! 2. Write new generation to Redis hash\n6\t//! 3. Wait for all live pods to observe new generation (beacon check)\n7\t//! 4. Drain wait for stragglers\n8\t//! 5. `DELETE /keys/{previous_uid}` on all Meilisearch nodes\n9\t//! 6. Clear previous from Redis hash\n10\t\n11\tuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n12\tuse miroir_core::task_store::{RedisTaskStore, SearchUiScopedKey, TaskStore};\n13\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n14\tuse std::time::Duration;\n15\tuse tracing::{info, warn, error};\n16\t\n17\tuse crate::routes::indexes::MeilisearchClient;\n18\t\n19\t/// State for the scoped key rotation background task.\n20\t#[derive(Clone)]\n21\tpub struct ScopedKeyRotationState {\n22\t    pub config: Arc<MiroirConfig>,\n23\t    pub redis: RedisTaskStore,\n24\t    pub pod_id: String,\n25\t}\n26\t\n27\t/// Response body for the manual rotation endpoint.\n28\t#[derive(serde::Serialize)]\n29\tpub struct RotateScopedKeyResponse {\n30\t    pub status: String,\n31\t    pub index_uid: String,\n32\t    pub generation: i64,\n33\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n34\t    pub previous_uid_revoked: Option<String>,\n35\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n36\t    pub error: Option<String>,\n37\t}\n38\t\n39\t/// Request body for the manual rotation endpoint.\n40\t#[derive(serde::Deserialize)]\n41\tpub struct RotateScopedKeyRequest {\n42\t    /// If true, bypass the timing gate and rotate immediately.\n43\t    #[serde(default)]\n44\t    pub force: bool,\n45\t}\n46\t\n47\t/// Run the background scoped key rotation loop.\n48\tpub async fn run_scoped_key_rotator(state: ScopedKeyRotationState) {\n49\t    if !state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n50\t        return;\n51\t    }\n52\t\n53\t    let check_interval = Duration::from_secs(3600); // Check every hour\n54\t    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(check_interval);\n55\t\n56\t    info!(\"scoped key rotation background task started\");\n57\t\n58\t    loop {\n59\t        interval.tick().await;\n60\t\n61\t        // Refresh our own pod presence heartbeat\n62\t        if let Err(e) = state.redis.register_pod_presence(&state.pod_id) {\n63\t            warn!(pod_id = %state.pod_id, error = %e, \"failed to register pod presence\");\n64\t        }\n65\t\n66\t        // Check each index for rotation need with a per-index leader lease\n67\t        let indexes = discover_scoped_indexes(&state).await;\n68\t        for index_uid in &indexes {\n69\t            let lease_scope = format!(\"search_ui_key_rotation:{}\", index_uid);\n70\t            let lease_now = now_ms();\n71\t            let lease_ttl_ms = (state.config.leader_election.lease_ttl_s as i64) * 1000;\n72\t            let expires_at = lease_now + lease_ttl_ms;\n73\t\n74\t            match state.redis.try_acquire_leader_lease(&lease_scope, &state.pod_id, expires_at, lease_now) {\n75\t                Ok(true) => {}\n76\t                Ok(false) => continue, // Another pod holds the lease for this index\n77\t                Err(e) => {\n78\t                    warn!(index = %index_uid, error = %e, \"failed to acquire rotation leader lease\");\n79\t                    continue;\n80\t                }\n81\t            }\n82\t\n83\t            if let Err(e) = check_and_rotate(&state, index_uid, false).await {\n84\t                error!(index = %index_uid, error = %e, \"scoped key rotation failed\");\n85\t            }\n86\t\n87\t            // Renew the lease for this index\n88\t            let _ = state.redis.renew_leader_lease(\n89\t                &lease_scope,\n90\t                &state.pod_id,\n91\t                now_ms() + lease_ttl_ms,\n92\t            );\n93\t        }\n94\t    }\n95\t}\n96\t\n97\t/// Check if a scoped key needs rotation and perform it if so.\n98\tpub async fn check_and_rotate(\n99\t    state: &ScopedKeyRotationState,\n100\t    index_uid: &str,\n101\t    force: bool,\n102\t) -> Result<RotateScopedKeyResponse, String> {\n103\t    let current = state.redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(index_uid)\n104\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"redis read failed: {e}\"))?;\n105\t\n106\t    // Timing gate check (skip if force)\n107\t    if !force {\n108\t        if !should_rotate(&current, &state.config) {\n109\t            return Ok(RotateScopedKeyResponse {\n110\t                status: \"skipped\".into(),\n111\t                index_uid: index_uid.into(),\n112\t                generation: current.as_ref().map(|k| k.generation).unwrap_or(0),\n113\t                previous_uid_revoked: None,\n114\t                error: None,\n115\t            });\n116\t        }\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    // Step 1: Mint new scoped key via Meilisearch POST /keys\n120\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(state.config.node_master_key.clone());\n121\t    let (new_key, new_uid) = mint_scoped_key(&client, &state.config, index_uid).await?;\n122\t\n123\t    // Step 2: Write new generation to Redis\n124\t    let new_generation = current.as_ref().map(|k| k.generation + 1).unwrap_or(1);\n125\t    let previous_uid = current.as_ref().map(|k| k.primary_uid.clone());\n126\t    let previous_key = current.as_ref().map(|k| k.primary_key.clone());\n127\t\n128\t    let scoped_key = SearchUiScopedKey {\n129\t        index_uid: index_uid.into(),\n130\t        primary_key: new_key.clone(),\n131\t        primary_uid: new_uid.clone(),\n132\t        previous_key,\n133\t        previous_uid: previous_uid.clone(),\n134\t        rotated_at: now_ms(),\n135\t        generation: new_generation,\n136\t    };\n137\t\n138\t    state.redis.set_search_ui_scoped_key(&scoped_key)\n139\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"redis write failed: {e}\"))?;\n140\t\n141\t    info!(\n142\t        index = %index_uid,\n143\t        generation = new_generation,\n144\t        \"new scoped key minted, waiting for pod observation\"\n145\t    );\n146\t\n147\t    // Step 3: Observe our own beacon immediately\n148\t    state.redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n149\t        &state.pod_id,\n150\t        index_uid,\n151\t        new_generation,\n152\t    ).map_err(|e| format!(\"beacon write failed: {e}\"))?;\n153\t\n154\t    // Step 4: Wait for drain period and check beacons\n155\t    let drain_s = state.config.search_ui.scoped_key_rotation_drain_s;\n156\t    let drain_deadline = tokio::time::Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(drain_s);\n157\t\n158\t    let mut previous_revoked: Option<String> = None;\n159\t\n160\t    loop {\n161\t        // Get live pods\n162\t        let live_pods = state.redis.get_live_pods()\n163\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"get_live_pods failed: {e}\"))?;\n164\t\n165\t        // Check if all live pods have observed the new generation\n166\t        let (all_observed, unobserved) = state.redis.check_scoped_key_observation(\n167\t            index_uid,\n168\t            new_generation,\n169\t            &live_pods,\n170\t        ).map_err(|e| format!(\"beacon check failed: {e}\"))?;\n171\t\n172\t        if all_observed {\n173\t            info!(\n174\t                index = %index_uid,\n175\t                generation = new_generation,\n176\t                \"all live pods observed new generation, revoking previous key\"\n177\t            );\n178\t\n179\t            // Step 6: Delete previous key from Meilisearch and clear from Redis\n180\t            if let Some(ref prev_uid) = previous_uid {\n181\t                if let Err(e) = revoke_previous_key(&client, &state.config, prev_uid).await {\n182\t                    warn!(previous_uid = %prev_uid, error = %e, \"failed to revoke previous key, will retry\");\n183\t                } else {\n184\t                    previous_revoked = Some(prev_uid.clone());\n185\t                    // Clear previous from Redis\n186\t                    if let Err(e) = state.redis.clear_scoped_key_previous(index_uid) {\n187\t                        warn!(error = %e, \"failed to clear previous key from redis\");\n188\t                    }\n189\t                    info!(index = %index_uid, previous_uid = %prev_uid, \"previous scoped key revoked\");\n190\t                }\n191\t            }\n192\t\n193\t            return Ok(RotateScopedKeyResponse {\n194\t                status: \"rotated\".into(),\n195\t                index_uid: index_uid.into(),\n196\t                generation: new_generation,\n197\t                previous_uid_revoked: previous_revoked,\n198\t                error: None,\n199\t            });\n200\t        }\n201\t\n202\t        // Not all pods have caught up yet\n203\t        if tokio::time::Instant::now() >= drain_deadline {\n204\t            warn!(\n205\t                index = %index_uid,\n206\t                generation = new_generation,\n207\t                unobserved = ?unobserved,\n208\t                \"drain wait expired, {} pods still unobserved — will retry on next tick\",\n209\t                unobserved.len()\n210\t            );\n211\t\n212\t            return Ok(RotateScopedKeyResponse {\n213\t                status: \"drain_pending\".into(),\n214\t                index_uid: index_uid.into(),\n215\t                generation: new_generation,\n216\t                previous_uid_revoked: None,\n217\t                error: Some(format!(\n218\t                    \"drain wait expired: {} pods unobserved: {}\",\n219\t                    unobserved.len(),\n220\t                    unobserved.join(\", \")\n221\t                )),\n222\t            });\n223\t        }\n224\t\n225\t        // Wait before rechecking (every 10 seconds)\n226\t        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(10)).await;\n227\t    }\n228\t}\n229\t\n230\t/// Check if rotation should happen based on timing gate.\n231\tfn should_rotate(current: &Option<SearchUiScopedKey>, config: &MiroirConfig) -> bool {\n232\t    let Some(key) = current else {\n233\t        // No key exists yet — need to mint initial key\n234\t        return true;\n235\t    };\n236\t\n237\t    let max_age_ms = (config.search_ui.scoped_key_max_age_days as i64) * 24 * 3600 * 1000;\n238\t    let rotate_before_ms = (config.search_ui.scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days as i64) * 24 * 3600 * 1000;\n239\t    let age = now_ms() - key.rotated_at;\n240\t\n241\t    // Rotate if key has been alive long enough that it will expire within rotate_before_days\n242\t    age >= (max_age_ms - rotate_before_ms)\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t/// Mint a new scoped Meilisearch key via POST /keys on all nodes.\n246\tpub async fn mint_scoped_key(\n247\t    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n248\t    config: &MiroirConfig,\n249\t    index_uid: &str,\n250\t) -> Result<(String, String), String> {\n251\t    let description = format!(\"miroir search-ui scoped key for index {}\", index_uid);\n252\t    let body = serde_json::json!({\n253\t        \"description\": description,\n254\t        \"actions\": [\"search\"],\n255\t        \"indexes\": [index_uid],\n256\t        \"expiresAt\": null,\n257\t    });\n258\t\n259\t    let mut created_key: Option<String> = None;\n260\t    let mut created_uid: Option<String> = None;\n261\t    let mut errors = Vec::new();\n262\t\n263\t    for node in &config.nodes {\n264\t        match client.post_raw(&node.address, \"/keys\", &body).await {\n265\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n266\t                if created_key.is_none() {\n267\t                    let resp: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&text)\n268\t                        .map_err(|e| format!(\"parse key response: {e}\"))?;\n269\t                    created_key = resp.get(\"key\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(String::from);\n270\t                    created_uid = resp.get(\"uid\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(String::from);\n271\t                }\n272\t            }\n273\t            Ok((status, _text)) => {\n274\t                errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {}\", node.id, status));\n275\t            }\n276\t            Err(e) => {\n277\t                errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", node.id, e));\n278\t            }\n279\t        }\n280\t    }\n281\t\n282\t    let key = created_key.ok_or_else(|| format!(\"failed to mint key on any node: {}\", errors.join(\"; \")))?;\n283\t    let uid = created_uid.ok_or_else(|| String::from(\"key created but no uid returned\"))?;\n284\t\n285\t    Ok((key, uid))\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\t/// Revoke a previous scoped key from all Meilisearch nodes.\n289\tpub async fn revoke_previous_key(\n290\t    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n291\t    config: &MiroirConfig,\n292\t    previous_uid: &str,\n293\t) -> Result<(), String> {\n294\t    let path = format!(\"/keys/{}\", previous_uid);\n295\t    let mut errors = Vec::new();\n296\t\n297\t    for node in &config.nodes {\n298\t        match client.delete_raw(&node.address, &path).await {\n299\t            Ok((_status, _text)) if _status >= 200 && _status < 300 => {}\n300\t            Ok((status, _text)) => {\n301\t                // 404 is fine — key was already revoked or never existed\n302\t                if status != 404 {\n303\t                    errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {}\", node.id, status));\n304\t                }\n305\t            }\n306\t            Err(e) => {\n307\t                errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", node.id, {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_88af8e4dbd7a466b9cbfb57b","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Bearer-token dispatch per plan §5 rules 0–5.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Three token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously:\n4\t//! the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them\n5\t//! deterministically in the order specified by §5.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! JWT signing-secret rotation (plan §9):\n8\t//! - Primary secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET`) signs new tokens; `kid` header identifies it.\n9\t//! - Optional previous secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS`) is present only during\n10\t//!   the rotation overlap window. Validation accepts either secret.\n11\t\n12\tuse axum::{\n13\t    extract::{FromRef, Request, State},\n14\t    http::{HeaderMap, Method},\n15\t    middleware::Next,\n16\t    response::{IntoResponse, Response},\n17\t};\n18\tuse base64::{engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD, Engine as _};\n19\tuse dashmap::DashMap;\n20\tuse hmac::{Hmac, Mac};\n21\tuse miroir_core::{task_store::TaskStore, MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\n22\tuse prometheus::Counter;\n23\tuse rand::RngCore;\n24\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n25\tuse sha2::Sha256;\n26\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n27\tuse subtle::ConstantTimeEq;\n28\t\n29\tuse crate::admin_session::{self, SealKey};\n30\t\n31\ttype HmacSha256 = Hmac<Sha256>;\n32\t\n33\t/// Extension carried in the request after successful cookie unseal.\n34\t/// Handlers extract this to look up the session in the task store.\n35\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n36\tpub struct AdminSessionId(pub String);\n37\t\n38\t/// State for CSRF middleware, Option<miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore>,\n43\t}\n44\t\n45\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n46\t// JWT claims (plan §13.21)\n47\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n48\t\n49\t/// Claims embedded in a search UI JWT session token.\n50\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n51\tpub struct JwtClaims {\n52\t    /// Subject — user identifier or \"anonymous\".\n53\t    pub sub: String,\n54\t    /// Index this token grants access to.\n55\t    pub idx: String,\n56\t    /// Granted scope (e.g. \"search\").\n57\t    pub scope: String,\n58\t    /// Issued-at timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n59\t    pub iat: u64,\n60\t    /// Expiration timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n61\t    pub exp: u64,\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t/// Key ID embedded in the JWT header to identify which secret signed it.\n65\tconst KID_PRIMARY: &str = \"primary\";\n66\tconst KID_PREVIOUS: &str = \"previous\";\n67\t\n68\t/// JWT header (always HS256).\n69\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n70\tstruct JwtHeader {\n71\t    alg: String,\n72\t    kid: String,\n73\t    typ: String,\n74\t}\n75\t\n76\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n77\t// Minimal HS256 JWT encode / decode (no external JWT crate needed)\n78\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n79\t\n80\t/// Encode and sign a JWT with the given secret.\n81\tfn jwt_encode(header: &JwtHeader, claims: &JwtClaims, secret: &[u8]) -> Result<String, String> {\n82\t    let header_json = serde_json::to_string(header).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n83\t    let claims_json = serde_json::to_string(claims).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n84\t\n85\t    let header_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(header_json.as_bytes());\n86\t    let payload_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(claims_json.as_bytes());\n87\t\n88\t    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64);\n89\t\n90\t    let mut mac =\n91\t        HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).map_err(|e| format!(\"HMAC init: {}\", e))?;\n92\t    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n93\t    let sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n94\t    let sig_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(sig);\n95\t\n96\t    Ok(format!(\"{}.{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64, sig_b64))\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\t/// Decode and verify a JWT with the given secret. Returns (header, claims).\n100\tfn jwt_decode(\n101\t    token: &str,\n102\t    secret: &[u8],\n103\t) -> Result<(JwtHeader, JwtClaims), JwtValidationError> {\n104\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n105\t    if parts.len() != 3 {\n106\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    let header_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n110\t        .decode(parts[0])\n111\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n112\t    let header: JwtHeader =\n113\t        serde_json::from_slice(&header_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n114\t\n115\t    if header.alg != \"HS256\" {\n116\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    // Verify signature\n120\t    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n121\t    let mut mac = HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).unwrap();\n122\t    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n123\t    let expected_sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n124\t\n125\t    let actual_sig = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n126\t        .decode(parts[2])\n127\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)?;\n128\t\n129\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n130\t    let sig_valid: bool = actual_sig.ct_eq(&expected_sig).into();\n131\t    if !sig_valid {\n132\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature);\n133\t    }\n134\t\n135\t    // Decode claims\n136\t    let claims_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n137\t        .decode(parts[1])\n138\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n139\t    let claims: JwtClaims =\n140\t        serde_json::from_slice(&claims_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n141\t\n142\t    // Check expiration with 30s leeway\n143\t    let now = epoch_seconds();\n144\t    if claims.exp + 30 < now {\n145\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired);\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    Ok((header, claims))\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n152\t// Auth state (shared via axum State)\n153\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n154\t\n155\t/// Configuration needed by the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n156\t#[derive(Clone)]\n157\tpub struct AuthState {\n158\t    pub master_key: String,\n159\t    pub admin_key: String,\n160\t    /// HMAC secret for signing/validating search UI JWTs (primary).\n161\t    pub jwt_primary: Option<String>,\n162\t    /// &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n174\t        f.debug_struct(\"AuthState\")\n175\t            .field(\"master_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n176\t            .field(\"admin_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n177\t            .field(\"jwt_primary\", &self.jwt_primary.as_ref().map(|_| \"[set]\"))\n178\t            .field(\"jwt_previous\", &self.jwt_previous.as_ref().map(|_| }\n183\t}\n184\t\n185\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n186\t// JWT signing / validation helpers\n187\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n188\t\n189\timpl AuthState {\n190\t    /// Create a new signed JWT session token for the given index.\n191\t    /// Always signs with the primary secret; `kid` header identifies it.\n192\t    pub fn sign_jwt(&self, sub: &str, idx: &str, scope: &str, ttl_s: u64) -> Option<String> {\n193\t        let secret = self.jwt_primary.as_ref()?;\n194\t        let now = epoch_seconds();\n195\t        let claims = JwtClaims {\n196\t            sub: sub.to_string(),\n197\t            idx: idx.to_string(),\n198\t            scope: scope.to_string(),\n199\t            iat: now,\n200\t            exp: now + ttl_s,\n201\t        };\n202\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n203\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n204\t            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n205\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n206\t        };\n207\t        jwt_encode(&header, &claims, secret.as_bytes()).ok()\n208\t    }\n209\t\n210\t    /// Validate a JWT string against either the primary or previous secret.\n211\t    /// Returns the parsed claims if validation succeeds, or an error.\n212\t    pub fn validate_jwt(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, JwtValidationError> {\n213\t        // Try primary secret first\n214\t        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_primary {\n215\t            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n216\t                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n217\t                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n218\t                _ => {} // signature mismatch — try previous\n219\t            }\n220\t        }\n221\t\n222\t        // Try previous secret (rotation overlap window)\n223\t        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_previous {\n224\t            if secret.is_empty() {\n225\t                return Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty);\n226\t            }\n227\t            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n228\t                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n229\t                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n230\t                _ => {} // signature mismatch\n231\t            }\n232\t        }\n233\t\n234\t        Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)\n235\t    }\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t/// Errors returned by JWT validation.\n239\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n240\tpub enum JwtValidationError {\n241\t    /// Token structure is invalid or alg is not HS256.\n242\t    Malformed,\n243\t    /// HMAC signature did not match any loaded secret.\n244\t    InvalidSignature,\n245\t    /// Token has expired.\n246\t    Expired,\n247\t    /// `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS` is set to the empty string (leak response).\n248\t    PreviousSecretEmpty,\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n252\t// CSRF token generation (plan §9)\n253\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n254\t\n255\t/// Generate a cryptographically random CSRF token.\n256\t/// Returns a URL-safe base64-encoded 32-byte token.\n257\tpub fn generate_csrf_token() -> String {\n258\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 32];\n259\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n260\t    URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(&bytes)\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t/// Extract the CSRF token from the `X-CSRF-Token` header.\n264\tpub fn extract_csrf_token(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n265\t    headers.get(\"X-CSRF-Token\")?.to_str().ok().map(String::from)\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t/// Constant-time comparison of CSRF tokens.\n269\tpub fn constant_time_csrf_compare(token: &str, expected: &str) -> bool {\n270\t    constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), expected.as_bytes())\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t/// Validate a CSRF token against the expected session token.\n274\t/// Returns Ok(()) if the token matches, or a CsrfMismatch error.\n275\tpub fn validate_csrf_token(provided: &str, expected: &str) -> Result<(), MiroirCode> {\n276\t    if constant_time_csrf_compare(provided, expected) {\n277\t        Ok(())\n278\t    } else {\n279\t        Err(MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch)\n280\t    }\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n284\t// Origin validation (plan §9)\n285\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n286\t\n287\t/// Result of origin validation.\n288\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n289\tpub enum OriginVerdict {\n290\t    /// Origin is allowed.\n291\t    Allowed,\n292\t    /// Origin is not in the allowed list.\n293\t    Forbidden,\n294\t    /// No Origin/Referer header present (for same-origin requests).\n295\t    Missing,\n296\t}\n297\t\n298\t/// Validate the Origin header against the allowed origins list.\n299\t/// Handles special \"same-origin\" value by comparing against the Host header.\n300\tpub fn validate_origin(\n301\t    headers: &HeaderMap,\n302\t    allowed_origins: &[String],\n303\t    is_same_origin_by_default: bool,\n304\t) -> OriginVerdict {\n305\t    // Try Origin header first (preferred for POST/DELETE/PUT)\n306\t    let origin = headers.get(\"origin\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok());\n307\t\n308\t    // Fall back to Referer header (for navigational requests)\n309\t    let referer = origin.or_else(|| headers.get(\"referer\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()));\n310\t\n311\t    let provided_origin = match referer {\n312\t        Some(o) => o,\n313\t        None => {\n314\t            // No Origin or Referer header - for same-origin requests, this is acceptable\n315\t            return if is_same_origin_by_default {\n316\t                OriginVerdict::Missing\n317\t            } else {\n318\t                OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n319\t            };\n320\t        }\n321\t    };\n322\t\n323\t    // Strip path from Referer to get origin\n324\t    let provided_origin = if let Some(ref_hdr) = headers.get(\"referer\") {\n325\t        if let Ok(ref_val) = ref_hdr.to_str() {\n326\t            // Find the first '/' after \"https://\" (skip the first 8 chars: \"https://\")\n327\t            if let Some(idx) = ref_val.chars().enumerate().skip(8).find(|(_, c)| *c == '/').map(|(i, _)| i) {\n328\t                &ref_val[..idx]\n329\t            } else {\n330\t                ref_val\n331\t            }\n332\t        } else {\n333\t            provided_origin\n334\t        }\n335\t    } else {\n336\t        provided_origin\n337\t    };\n338\t\n339\t    // Check against allowed origins\n340\t    for allowed in allowed_origins {\n341\t        // Special \"same-origin\" value - compare against Host header\n342\t        if allowed == \"same-origin\" {\n343\t            if let Some(host) = headers.get(\"host\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()) {\n344\t                // Construct origin from scheme (https) and host\n345\t                let same_origin = format!(\"https://{}\", host);\n346\t                if provided_origin == same_origin || provided_origin == host {\n347\t                    return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n348\t                }\n349\t            }\n350\t        } else if allowed == \"*\" {\n351\t            // Wildcard allows any origin\n352\t            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n353\t        } else if provided_origin == allowed {\n354\t            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n355\t        }\n356\t    }\n357\t\n358\t    OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n359\t}\n360\t\n361\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n362\t// CSP header builder (plan §9)\n363\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n364\t\n365\t/// Build a CSP header value by merging base template with overrides.\n366\t/// Overrides are merged additively - they never replace the base template.\n367\tpub fn build_csp_header(\n368\t    base_template: &str,\n369\t    overrides: &miroir_core::config::CspOverridesConfig,\n370\t) -> String {\n371\t    let mut directives: Vec<(String, Vec<String>)> = base_template\n372\t        .split(';')\n373\t        .map(|s| s.trim())\n374\t        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())\n375\t        .map(|directive| {\n376\t            let parts: Vec<&str> = directive.splitn(2, ' ').collect();\n377\t            if parts.len() == 2 {\n378\t                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![parts[1].to_string()])\n379\t            } else {\n380\t                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![])\n381\t            }\n382\t        })\n383\t        .collect();\n384\t\n385\t    // Helper to merge overrides into a directive\n386\t    let merge_into = |directives: &mut Vec<(String, Vec<String>)>,\n387\t                      name: &str,\n388\t                      values: &[String]| {\n389\t        if values.is_empty() {\n390\t            return;\n391\t        }\n392\t        let name_lower = name.to_lowercase();\n393\t        if let Some(entry) = directives.iter_mut().find(|(n, _)| n == &name_lower) {\n394\t            // Append to existing directive\n395\t            entry.1.extend(values.iter().cloned());\n396\t            entry.1.dedup(); // Remove duplicates\n397\t        } else {\n398\t            // Add new directive\n399\t            directives.push((name_lower, values.to_vec()));\n400\t        }\n401\t    };\n402\t\n403\t    // Merge each override category\n404\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"script-src\", &overrides.script_src);\n405\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"img-src\", &overrides.img_src);\n406\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"connect-src\", &overrides.connect_src);\n407\t\n408\t    // Rebuild CSP string\n409\t    directives\n410\t        .into_iter()\n411\t        .map(|(name, values)| {\n412\t            if values.is_empty() {\n413\t                name\n414\t            } else {\n415\t                format!(\"{} {}\", name, values.join(\" \"))\n416\t            }\n417\t        })\n418\t        .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n419\t        .join(\"; \")\n420\t}\n421\t\n422\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n423\t// Dispatch verdict\n424\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n425\t\n426\t/// Result of the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n427\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n428\tpub enum AuthVerdict {\n429\t    /// Request is dispatch-exempt (rule 0); handler decides auth.\n430\t    Exempt,\n431\t    /// Authenticated with the given token kind.\n432\t    Authenticated(TokenKind),\n433\t    /// Bearer token looked like a JWT but failed validation (rule 1).\n434\t    JwtInvalid,\n435\t    /// JWT was signature-valid but scope was insufficient (rule 1).\n436\t    JwtScopeDenied,\n437\t    /// No matching key / missing Authorization (rule 4).\n438\t    InvalidAuth,\n439\t}\n440\t\n441\t/// Which key or token type satisfied authentication.\n442\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n443\tpub enum TokenKind {\n444\t    MasterKey,\n445\t    AdminKey,\n446\t    /// JWT validated against primary or previous secret.\n447\t    Jwt,\n448\t    /// Admin session cookie — sealed session ID validated against task store.\n449\t    AdminSession,\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\timpl AuthVerdict {\n453\t    pub fn is_allowed(&self) -> bool {\n454\t        matches!(self, AuthVerdict::Exempt | AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_))\n455\t    }\n456\t}\n457\t\n458\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n459\t// Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt check\n460\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n461\t\n462\t/// Returns true when `(method, path)` is in the exhaustive dispatch-exempt list\n463\t/// (plan §5 rule 5). Exempt endpoints run their handler directly; rules 1–4\n464\t/// are never consulted.\n465\tpub fn is_dispatch_exempt(method: &Method, path: &str) -> bool {\n466\t    // `GET /health` — unauthenticated liveness probe (Meilisearch-compatible)\n467\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/health\" {\n468\t        return true;\n469\t    }\n470\t\n471\t    // `GET /version` — unauthenticated version endpoint (Meilisearch-compatible)\n472\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/version\" {\n473\t        return true;\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    // `GET /_miroir/ready` — unauthenticated readiness probe (plan §10)\n477\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/_miroir/ready\" {\n478\t        return true;\n479\t    }\n480\t\n481\t    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch\n482\t    if method == Method::GET {\n483\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/\") {\n484\t            // Must have at least one path segment after the prefix\n485\t            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains(\"//\");\n486\t        }\n487\t    }\n488\t\n489\t    // `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body\n490\t    if method == Method::POST && path == \"/_miroir/admin/login\" {\n491\t        return true;\n492\t    }\n493\t\n494\t    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per search_ui.auth.mode\n495\t    if method == Method::GET {\n496\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/\") {\n497\t            let segments: Vec<&str> = rest.split('/').collect();\n498\t            if segments.len() == 2 && segments[1] == \"session\" && !segments[0].is_empty() {\n499\t                return true;\n500\t            }\n501\t        }\n502\t    }\n503\t\n504\t    // `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA entry point\n505\t    if method == Method::GET {\n506\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/ui/search/\") {\n507\t            // Single non-empty segment (the index name)\n508\t            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains('/');\n509\t        }\n510\t    }\n511\t\n512\t    false\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n516\t// Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe\n517\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n518\t\n519\t/// Returns true if `token` has the structural shape of a JWT (three\n520\t/// dot-separated base64url segments).\n521\tpub fn probe_jwt_shape(token: &str) -> bool {\n522\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n523\t    if parts.len() != 3 {\n524\t        return false;\n525\t    }\n526\t    // Each segment should be non-empty and look like base64url\n527\t    parts.iter().all(|s| {\n528\t        !s.is_empty()\n529\t            && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '-' || c == '_' || c == '=')\n530\t    })\n531\t}\n532\t\n533\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n534\t// Helper — admin path check\n535\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n536\t\n537\t/// Returns true if `path` starts with `/_miroir/` (admin surface).\n538\tpub fn is_admin_path(path: &str) -> bool {\n539\t    path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\")\n540\t}\n541\t\n542\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n543\t// Constant-time opaque key comparison\n544\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n545\t\n546\t/// Constant-time comparison of an opaque token against an expected key.\n547\t/// Prevents timing side-channels on secret key values.\n548\tpub fn constant_time_compare(token: &[u8], expected: &[u8]) -> bool {\n549\t    token.ct_eq(expected).into()\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n553\t// Core dispatch — rules 0–4\n554\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n555\t\n556\t/// Execute the full bearer-token dispatch chain for a request.\n557\t///\n558\t/// `bearer_token` is the raw value after stripping `\"Bearer \"` from the\n559\t/// `Authorization` header (may be `None` if the header is absent).\n560\tpub fn dispatch_bearer(\n561\t    method: &Method,\n562\t    path: &str,\n563\t    bearer_token: Option<&str>,\n564\t    state: &AuthState,\n565\t) -> AuthVerdict {\n566\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt endpoints skip all auth checks\n567\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(method, path) {\n568\t        return AuthVerdict::Exempt;\n569\t    }\n570\t\n571\t    let token = match bearer_token {\n572\t        Some(t) => t,\n573\t        None => return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth, // Rule 4 — missing auth\n574\t    };\n575\t\n576\t    // Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe, then full validation\n577\t    if probe_jwt_shape(token) {\n578\t        match state.validate_jwt(token) {\n579\t            Ok(_claims) => return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt),\n580\t            Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty) => {\n581\t                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n582\t            }\n583\t            Err(_) => {\n584\t                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n585\t            }\n586\t        }\n587\t    }\n588\t\n589\t    // Rule 2 — admin-path opaque-token match\n590\t    if is_admin_path(path) {\n591\t        if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n592\t            return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::AdminKey);\n593\t        }\n594\t        return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth; // Rule 4\n595\t    }\n596\t\n597\t    // Rule 3 — master-key match (non-admin paths)\n598\t    if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.master_key.as_bytes()) {\n599\t        return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::MasterKey);\n600\t    }\n601\t\n602\t    // Rule 4 — mismatch\n603\t    AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n607\t// X-Admin-Key short-circuit\n608\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n609\t\n610\t/// Check the `X-Admin-Key` header for admin endpoints.\n611\t/// Returns `true` if the header is present and matches `admin_key`.\n612\t/// Evaluated independently of the bearer chain — short-circuits directly.\n613\tpub fn check_x_admin_key(headers: &HeaderMap, admin_key: &[u8]) -> bool {\n614\t    match headers.get(\"X-Admin-Key\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()) {\n615\t        Some(key) => constant_time_compare(key.as_bytes(), admin_key),\n616\t        None => false,\n617\t    }\n618\t}\n619\t\n620\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n621\t// Axum middleware\n622\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n623\t\n624\t/// Extract the bearer token from `Authorization: Bearer <value>`.\n625\tfn extract_bearer(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<&str> {\n626\t    let auth = headers.get(\"authorization\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n627\t    auth.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n628\t}\n629\t\n630\t/// Extract the sealed admin session cookie value from the Cookie header.\n631\tpub fn extract_admin_session_cookie(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n632\t    let cookie_header = headers.get(\"cookie\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n633\t    for pair in cookie_header.split(';') {\n634\t        let pair = pair.trim();\n635\t        if let Some(value) = pair.strip_prefix(&format!(\"{}=\", admin_session::COOKIE_NAME)) {\n636\t            return Some(value.to_string());\n637\t        }\n638\t    }\n639\t    None\n640\t}\n641\t\n642\t/// Unseal an admin session cookie, returning the session ID.\n643\tpub fn unseal_admin_cookie(cookie_value: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<String, admin_session::SealError> {\n644\t    admin_session::unseal_session(cookie_value, key)\n645\t}\n646\t\n647\t/// Axum middleware implementing the bearer-token dispatch chain (plan §5).\n648\tpub async fn auth_middleware(\n649\t    State(state): State<AuthState>,\n650\t    req: Request,\n651\t    next: Next,\n652\t) -> Response {\n653\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n654\t    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n655\t\n656\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt: skip everything, handler decides auth\n657\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n658\t        return next.run(req).await;\n659\t    }\n660\t\n661\t    // X-Admin-Key short-circuit for admin endpoints\n662\t    if is_admin_path(&path) && check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n663\t        return next.run(req).await;\n664\t    }\n665\t\n666\t    // Admin session cookie check for admin endpoints (plan §9, §13.19).\n667\t    // If a sealed admin session cookie is present, unseal it and authenticate.\n668\t    // Revoked sessions are rejected immediately.\n669\t    if is_admin_path(&path) {\n670\t        if let Some(cookie_value) = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()) {\n671\t            match unseal_admin_cookie(&cookie_value, &state.seal_key) {\n672\t                Ok(session_id) => {\n673\t                    // Check revocation cache (populated on logout + Pub/Sub).\n674\t                    if state.revoked_sessions.contains_key(&session_id) {\n675\t                        return MeilisearchError::new(\n676\t                            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n677\t                            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n678\t                        )\n679\t                        .into_response();\n680\t                    }\n681\t                    let mut req = req;\n682\t                    req.extensions_mut().insert(AdminSessionId(session_id));\n683\t                    return next.run(req).await;\n684\t                }\n685\t                Err(e) => {\n686\t                    // Cookie tampering or wrong seal key (e.g. cross-pod key\n687\t                    // mismatch in HA).  Log a warning so operators can diagnose\n688\t                    // ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY divergence across pods.\n689\t                    tracing::warn!(\n690\t                        path = %path,\n691\t                        error = %e,\n692\t                        \"admin session cookie unseal failed — tampered cookie or cross-pod key mismatch\"\n693\t                    );\n694\t                }\n695\t            }\n696\t        }\n697\t    }\n698\t\n699\t    // Extract bearer token\n700\t    let bearer = extract_bearer(req.headers());\n701\t\n702\t    // Run the dispatch chain\n703\t    let verdict = dispatch_bearer(&method, &path, bearer, &state);\n704\t\n705\t    match verdict {\n706\t        AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_) | AuthVerdict::Exempt => next.run(req).await,\n707\t        AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid => MeilisearchError::new(\n708\t            MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n709\t            \"The provided JWT is invalid or expired.\",\n710\t        )\n711\t        .into_response(),\n712\t        AuthVerdict::JwtScopeDenied => MeilisearchError::new(\n713\t            MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n714\t            \"The provided JWT does not grant access to this resource.\",\n715\t        )\n716\t        .into_response(),\n717\t        AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth => MeilisearchError::new(\n718\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n719\t            \"The provided authorization is invalid.\",\n720\t        )\n721\t        .into_response(),\n722\t    }\n723\t}\n724\t\n725\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n726\t// CSRF validation middleware (plan §9)\n727\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n728\t\n729\t/// CSRF middleware that validates `X-CSRF-Token` on state-changing requests.\n730\t///\n731\t/// Bypasses CSRF check when:\n732\t/// - Request is authenticated via Bearer token (not admin session cookie)\n733\t/// - X-Admin-Key header is present\n734\t/// - Request method is safe (GET, HEAD, OPTIONS)\n735\t/// - Path is dispatch-exempt\n736\t///\n737\t/// For admin session cookie auth, requires `X-CSRF-Token` header to match\n738\t/// the token stored in the session.\n739\tpub async fn csrf_middleware(\n740\t    State(state): State<CsrfState>,\n741\t    req: Request,\n742\t    next: Next,\n743\t) -> Response {\n744\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n745\t    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n746\t\n747\t    // Skip CSRF for safe methods\n748\t    if matches!(method, Method::GET | Method::HEAD | Method::OPTIONS) {\n749\t        return next.run(req).await;\n750\t    }\n751\t\n752\t    // Skip CSRF for non-admin paths\n753\t    if !is_admin_path(&path) {\n754\t        return next.run(req).await;\n755\t    }\n756\t\n757\t    // Skip CSRF for dispatch-exempt endpoints\n758\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n759\t        return next.run(req).await;\n760\t    }\n761\t\n762\t    // Skip CSRF if X-Admin-Key is present (bypasses CSRF)\n763\t    if check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.auth.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n764\t        return next.run(req).await;\n765\t    }\n766\t\n767\t    // Check if authenticated via admin session cookie\n768\t    let has_session_cookie = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()).is_some();\n769\t    let has_bearer_token = extract_bearer(req.headers()).is_some();\n770\t\n771\t    // CSRF only applies to session-cookie auth, not bearer tokens\n772\t    if !has_session_cookie || has_bearer_token {\n773\t        return next.run(req).await;\n774\t    }\n775\t\n776\t    // Extract CSRF token from header\n777\t    let csrf_token = match extract_csrf_token(req.headers()) {\n778\t        Some(token) => token,\n779\t        None => {\n780\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n781\t                MiroirCode::MissingCsrf,\n782\t                \"CSRF token is required for state-changing requests.\",\n783\t            )\n784\t            .into_response();\n785\t        }\n786\t    };\n787\t\n788\t    // Get session ID from extensions (set by auth_middleware)\n789\t    let session_id = match req.extensions().get::<AdminSessionId>() {\n790\t        Some(id) => id.0.clone(),\n791\t        None => {\n792\t            // Session cookie was present but auth_middleware didn't set AdminSessionId\n793\t            // This means the session was invalid/expired/revoked\n794\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n795\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n796\t                \"Admin session is invalid or expired.\",\n797\t            )\n798\t            .into_response();\n799\t        }\n800\t    };\n801\t\n802\t    // Validate CSRF token against session\n803\t    let Some(redis_store) = state.redis_store.as_ref() else {\n804\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n805\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n806\t            \"Admin sessions require Redis task store.\",\n807\t        )\n808\t        .into_response();\n809\t    };\n810\t\n811\t    let session = match redis_store.get_admin_session(&session_id) {\n812\t        Ok(Some(s)) => s,\n813\t        Ok(None) => {\n814\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n815\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n816\t                \"Admin session not found.\",\n817\t            )\n818\t            .into_response();\n819\t        }\n820\t        Err(e) => {\n821\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, session_prefix = &session_id[..session_id.len().min(8)], \"failed to get admin session for CSRF validation\");\n822\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n823\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n824\t                \"Failed to validate session.\",\n825\t            )\n826\t            .into_response();\n827\t        }\n828\t    };\n829\t\n830\t    // Check if revoked\n831\t    if session.revoked {\n832\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n833\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n834\t            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n835\t        )\n836\t        .into_response();\n837\t    }\n838\t\n839\t    // Check expiration\n840\t    let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n841\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n842\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n843\t        .as_secs() as i64;\n844\t    if session.expires_at < now {\n845\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n846\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n847\t            \"Admin session has expired.\",\n848\t        )\n849\t        .into_response();\n850\t    }\n851\t\n852\t    // Constant-time compare CSRF tokens\n853\t    if !constant_time_csrf_compare(&csrf_token, &session.csrf_token) {\n854\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n855\t            MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch,\n856\t            \"CSRF token does not match the session token.\",\n857\t        )\n858\t        .into_response();\n859\t    }\n860\t\n861\t    next.run(req).await\n862\t}\n863\t\n864\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n865\t// Rate-limit hook types (Phase 2 in-memory stub, Phase 6 multi-pod)\n866\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n867\t\n868\t/// Rate-limit bucket key types wired into the dispatch chain.\n869\t/// Phase 2 keeps these as in-memory counters; Phase 6 will back them\n870\t/// with the task store (Redis/SQLite).\n871\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n872\tpub enum RateLimitBucket {\n873\t    /// `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`\n874\t    AdminLogin(String),\n875\t    /// `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>`\n876\t    SearchUi(String),\n877\t}\n878\t\n879\t/// In-memory rate limiter (Phase 2 stub). Always returns `Ok(())` — actual\n880\t/// enforcement is deferred to Phase 6 multi-pod. The hook is wired here so\n881\t/// handlers can call `limiter.check()` without cfg-gating.\n882\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n883\tpub struct RateLimiter;\n884\t\n885\timpl RateLimiter {\n886\t    pub fn check(&self, _bucket: &RateLimitBucket) -> Result<(), ()> {\n887\t        Ok(()) // Phase 2: always allow\n888\t    }\n889\t}\n890\t\n891\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n892\t// Helpers\n893\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n894\t\n895\tfn epoch_seconds() -> u64 {\n896\t    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n897\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n898\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n899\t        .as_secs()\n900\t}\n901\t\n902\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n903\t// Tests\n904\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n905\t\n906\t#[cfg(test)]\n907\tmod tests {\n908\t    use super::*;\n909\t\n910\t    fn test_key() -> SealKey {\n911\t        SealKey::from_bytes([42u8; 32])\n912\t    }\n913\t\n914\t    fn test_state() -> AuthState {\n915\t        AuthState {\n916\t            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n917\t            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n918\t            jwt_primary: None,\n919\t            jwt_previous: None,\n920\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n921\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n922\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n923\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n924\t            ).unwrap(),\n925\t        }\n926\t    }\n927\t\n928\t    fn test_state_with_jwt() -> AuthState {\n929\t        AuthState {\n930\t            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n931\t            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n932\t            jwt_primary: Some(\"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\".to_string()),\n933\t            jwt_previous: None,\n934\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n935\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n936\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n937\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n938\t            ).unwrap(),\n939\t        }\n940\t    }\n941\t\n942\t    fn test_state_with_dual_jwt() -> AuthState {\n943\t        AuthState {\n944\t            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n945\t            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n946\t            jwt_primary: Some(\"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\".to_string()),\n947\t            jwt_previous: Some(\"test-secret-previous-key-32byte\".to_string()),\n948\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n949\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n950\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n951\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n952\t            ).unwrap(),\n953\t        }\n954\t    }\n955\t\n956\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n957\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt tests\n958\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n959\t\n960\t    #[test]\n961\t    fn get_metrics_requires_admin_key() {\n962\t        assert!(!is_dispatch_exempt(&Method::GET, \"/_miroir/metrics\"));\n963\t        assert!(!is_dispatch_exempt(&Method::POST, \"/_miroir/metrics\"));\n964\t    }\n965\t\n966\t    #[test]\n967\t    fn exempt_get_locale_star() {\n968\t        assert!(is_dispatch_exempt(&Method::GET, \"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/en-US\"));\n969\t        assert!(is_dispatch_exempt(&Method::GET, \"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/fr\"));\n970\t    }\n971\t\n972\t    #[test]\n973\t    fn exempt_get_locale_no_variant_not_exempt() {\n974\t        assert!(!is_dispatch_exempt(&Method::GET, \"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/\"));\n975\t    }\n976\t\n977\t    #[test]\n978\t    fn exempt_post_admin_login() {\n979\t        assert!(is_dispatch_exempt(&Method::POST, \"/_miroir/admin/login\"));\n980\t    }\n981\t\n982\t    #[test]\n983\t    fn exempt_get_admin_login_not_exempt() {\n984\t        assert!(!is_dispatch_exempt(&Method::GET, \"/_miroir/admin/login\"));\n985\t    }\n986\t\n987\t    #[test]\n988\t    fn exempt_get_session() {\n989\t        assert!(is_dispatch_exempt(&Method::GET, \"/_miroir/ui/search/products/session\"));\n990\t    }\n991\t\n992\t    #[test]\n993\t    fn exempt_get_session_no_index_not_exempt() {\n994\t        assert!(!is_dispatch_exempt(&Method::GET, \"/_miroir/ui/search//session\"));\n995\t    }\n996\t\n997\t    #[test]\n998\t    fn exempt_get_search_ui_spa() {\n999\t        assert!(is_dispatch_exempt(&Method::GET, \"/ui/search/products\"));\n1000\t    }\n1001\t\n1002\t    #[test]\n1003\t    fn exempt_get_search_ui_no_index_not_exempt() {\n1004\t {\n1215\t        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n1216\t        let now = epoch_seconds();\n1217\t        let claims = JwtClaims {\n1218\t            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n1219\t            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n1220\t            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n1221\t            iat: now - 3600,\n1222\t            exp: now - 100, // expired well beyond 30s leeway\n1223\t        };\n1224\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n1225\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n1226\t            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n1227\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n1228\t        };\n1229\t        let token = jwt_encode(\n1230\t            &header,\n1231\t            &claims,\n1232\t            state.jwt_primary.as_ref().unwrap().as_bytes(),\n1233\t        )\n1234\t        .unwrap();\n1235\t\n1236\t        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n1237\t        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::Expired));\n1238\t    }\n1239\t\n1240\t    #[test]\n1241\t    fn tampered_signature_returns_invalid_signature() {\n1242\t        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n1243\t        let mut token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n1244\t        // Tamper with the signature\n1245\t        let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n1246\t        token = format!(\"{}.{}.tampered_sig\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n1247\t\n1248\t        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n1249\t        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature));\n1250\t    }\n1251\t\n1252\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1253\t    // JWT dual-secret rotation validation\n1254\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1255\t\n1256\t    #[test]\n1257\t    fn rotation_old_token_validates_via_previous_secret() {\n1258\t        let primary = \"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\";\n1259\t        let previous = \"test-secret-previous-key-32byte\";\n1260\t\n1261\t        // Sign token with the previous secret\n1262\t        let now = epoch_seconds();\n1263\t        let claims = JwtClaims {\n1264\t            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n1265\t            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n1266\t            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n1267\t            iat: now,\n1268\t            exp: now + 900,\n1269\t        };\n1270\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n1271\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n1272\t            kid: KID_PREVIOUS.to_string(),\n1273\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n1274\t        };\n1275\t        let old_token = jwt_encode(&header, &claims, previous.as_bytes()).unwrap();\n1276\t\n1277\t        // Simulate rotation — new primary, old primary as previous\n1278\t        let state = AuthState {\n1279\t            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n1280\t            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n1281\t            jwt_primary: Some(primary.to_string()),\n1282\t            jwt_previous: Some(previous.to_string()),\n1283\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n1284\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n1285\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n1286\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n1287\t            ).unwrap(),\n1288\t        };\n1289\t\n1290\t        // Old token should still validate via previous secret\n1291\t        let validated = state.validate_jwt(&old_token).unwrap();\n1292\t        assert_eq!(validated.sub, \"user1\");\n1293\t\n1294\t        // And dispatch should authenticate it\n1295\t        let verdict = assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt));\n1302\t    }\n1303\t\n1304\t    #[test]\n1305\t    fn rotation_new_token_validates_via_primary_secret() {\n1306\t        let state =          ).unwrap(),\n1365\t        };\n1366\t\n1367\t        let result = state.validate_jwt(\"eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJ0ZXN0In0.fake\");\n1368\t        assert_eq!(result, \"final-primary-secret-key-32bytes\";\n1374\t        let state = AuthState {\n1375\t            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n1376\t            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n1377\t -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1413\t    // End-to-end rotation scenario\n1414\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1415\t\n1416\t    #[test]\n1417\t    fn full_rotation_e2e() {\n1418\t        let secret_v1 = \"version-1-secret-key-32bytes-long!\";\n1419\t        let secret_v2 = \"version-2-secret-key-32bytes-long!\";\n1420\t\n1421\t        // Pre-rotation: only v1\n1422\t        let pre = AuthState {\n1423\t            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n1424\t            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n1425\t            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v1.into()),\n1426\t            jwt_previous: None,\n1427\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n1428\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n1429\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n1430\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n1431\t            ).unwrap(),\n1432\t        };\n1433\t        let token_v1 = pre.sign_jwt(\"alice\", \"idx\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n1434\t        assert!(pre.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_ok());\n1435\t\n1436\t        // During rotation: v2 primary, v1 previous\n1437\t        let during = AuthState {\n1438\t            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n1439\t            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n1440\t            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v2.into()),\n1441\t            jwt_previous: Some(secret_v1.into()),\n1442\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n1443\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n1444\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n1445\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n1446\t            ).unwrap(),\n1447\t        };\n1448\t        // Old token still validates\n1449\t        assert!(during.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_ok());\n1450\t        // New tokens work too\n1451\t        let token_v2 = during.sign_jwt(\"bob\", \"idx\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n1452\t        assert!(during.validate_jwt(&token_v2).is_ok());\n1453\t\n1454\t        // Post-rotation: only v2\n1455\t        let post = AuthState {\n1456\t            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n1457\t            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n1458\t            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v2.into()),\n1459\t            jwt_previous: None,\n1460\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n1461\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n1462\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n1463\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n1464\t            ).unwrap(),\n1465\t        };\n1466\t        // New token still works\n1467\t        assert!(post.validate_jwt(&token_v2).is_ok());\n1468\t        // Old token is rejected (signed with v1, no previous loaded)\n1469\t        assert!(post.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_err());\n1470\t    }\n1471\t\n1472\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1473\t    // Rule 2 — admin-path opaque-token match (admin_key only)\n1474\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1475\t\n1476\t    #[test]\n1477\t    fn 200ms.\n93\t#[tokio::test]\n94\tasync fn test_cross_pod_revocation_via_pubsub() {\n95\t    let (store, redis_url) = redis_store().await;\n96\t\n97\t    // Simulate two pods, each with their own            );\n172\t        }\n173\t\n174\t        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n175\t    }\n176\t\n177\t    let elapsed = start.elapsed();\n178\t    assert!(\n179\t        elapsed < deadline,\n180\t        \"Propagation should be fast, took {:?}\",\n181\t        elapsed\n182\t    );\n183\t\n184\t    // Both pods must now reject the session\n185\t    assert!(\n186\t sub_b.abort();\n196\t}\n197\t\n198\t/// Multiple sessions: revoking one does not affect others.\n199\t#[tokio::test]\n200\tasync fn test_revocation_is_per_session() {\n201\t    let (store, _url) = &JwtClaims, secret: &[u8]) -> Result<String, String> {\n82\t    let header_json = serde_json::to_string(header).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n83\t    let claims_json = serde_json::to_string(claims).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n84\t\n85\t    let header_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(header_json.as_bytes());\n86\t    let payload_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(sig);\n95\t\n96\t    Ok(format!(\"{}.{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64, sig_b64))\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\t/// Decode and verify a JWT with the given secret. Returns (header, claims).\n100\tfn jwt_decode(\n101\t    token: &str,\n102\t    secret: &[u8],\n103\t) -> Result<(JwtHeader, JwtClaims), JwtValidationError> {\n104\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n105\t    if parts.len() != 3 {\n106\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    let header_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n110\t        .decode(parts[0])\n111\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n112\t    let header: JwtHeader =\n113\t        serde_json::from_slice(&header_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n114\t\n115\t    if header.alg != \"HS256\" {\n116\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    // Verify signature\n120\t    let
