subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n130\t    let sig_valid: bool = actual_sig.ct_eq(&expected_sig).into();\n131\t    if !sig_valid {\n132\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature);\n133\t    }\n134\t\n135\t    // Decode claims\n136\t    let claims_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n137\t        .decode(parts[1])\n138\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n139\t    let claims: JwtClaims =\n140\t        serde_json::from_slice(&claims_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n141\t\n142\t    // Check expiration with 30s leeway\n143\t    let now = epoch_seconds();\n144\t    if claims.exp + 30 < now {\n145\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired);\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    Ok((header, claims))\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n152\t// Auth state (shared via axum State)\n153\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n154\t\n155\t/// Configuration needed by the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n156\t#[derive(Clone)]\n157\tpub struct AuthState {\n158\t    pub master_key: String,\n159\t    pub admin_key: String,\n160\t    /// HMAC secret for signing/validating search UI JWTs (primary).\n161\t    pub jwt_primary: Option<String>,\n162\t    /// Optional previous secret active during rotation overlap window.\n163\t    pub jwt_previous: Option<String>,\n164\t    /// Key for sealing/unsealing admin session cookies (XChaCha20-Poly1305).\n165\t    pub seal_key: SealKey,\n166\t    /// In-memory set of revoked admin session IDs (populated on logout, Pub/Sub).\n167\t    pub revoked_sessions: Arc<DashMap<String, ()>>,\n168\t    /// Counter for revoked admin sessions (miroir_admin_session_revoked_total).\n169\t    pub admin_session_revoked_total: Counter,\n170\t}\n171\t\n172\timpl std::fmt::Debug for AuthState {\n173\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n174\t        f.debug_struct(\"AuthState\")\n175\t            .field(\"master_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n176\t            .field(\"admin_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n177\t            .field(\"jwt_primary\", &self.jwt_primary.as_ref().map(|_| \"[set]\"))\n178\t            .field(\"jwt_previous\", &self.jwt_previous.as_ref().map(|_| \"[set]\"))\n179\t            .field(\"seal_key\", &self.seal_key)\n180\t            .field(\"revoked_sessions\", &self.revoked_sessions.len())\n181\t            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n182\t    }\n183\t}\n184\t\n185\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n186\t// JWT signing / validation helpers\n187\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n188\t\n189\timpl AuthState {\n190\t    /// Create a new signed JWT session token for the given index.\n191\t    /// Always signs with the primary secret; `kid` header identifies it.\n192\t    pub fn sign_jwt(&self, sub: &str, idx: &str, scope: &str, ttl_s: u64) -> Option<String> {\n193\t        let secret = self.jwt_primary.as_ref()?;\n194\t        let now = epoch_seconds();\n195\t        let claims = JwtClaims {\n196\t            sub: sub.to_string(),\n197\t            idx: idx.to_string(),\n198\t            scope: scope.to_string(),\n199\t            iat: now,\n200\t            exp: now + ttl_s,\n201\t        };\n202\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n203\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n204\t            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n205\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n206\t        };\n207\t        jwt_encode(&header, &claims, secret.as_bytes()).ok()\n208\t    }\n209\t\n210\t    /// Validate a JWT string against either the primary or previous secret.\n211\t    /// Returns the parsed claims if validation succeeds, or an error.\n212\t    pub fn validate_jwt(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, JwtValidationError> {\n213\t        // Try primary secret first\n214\t        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_primary {\n215\t            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n216\t                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n217\t                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n218\t                _ => {} // signature mismatch — try previous\n219\t            }\n220\t        }\n221\t\n222\t        // Try previous secret (rotation overlap window)\n223\t        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_previous {\n224\t            if secret.is_empty() {\n225\t                return Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty);\n226\t            }\n227\t            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n228\t                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n229\t                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n230\t                _ => {} // signature mismatch\n231\t            }\n232\t        }\n233\t\n234\t        Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)\n235\t    }\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t/// Errors returned by JWT validation.\n239\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n240\tpub enum JwtValidationError {\n241\t    /// Token structure is invalid or alg is not HS256.\n242\t    Malformed,\n243\t    /// HMAC signature did not match any loaded secret.\n244\t    InvalidSignature,\n245\t    /// Token has expired.\n246\t    Expired,\n247\t    /// `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS` is set to the empty string (leak response).\n248\t    PreviousSecretEmpty,\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n252\t// CSRF token generation (plan §9)\n253\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n254\t\n255\t/// Generate a cryptographically random CSRF token.\n256\t/// Returns a URL-safe base64-encoded 32-byte token.\n257\tpub fn generate_csrf_token() -> String {\n258\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 32];\n259\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n260\t    URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(&bytes)\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t/// Extract the CSRF token from the `X-CSRF-Token` header.\n264\tpub fn extract_csrf_token(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n265\t    headers.get(\"X-CSRF-Token\")?.to_str().ok().map(String::from)\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t/// Constant-time comparison of CSRF tokens.\n269\tpub fn constant_time_csrf_compare(token: &str, expected: &str) -> bool {\n270\t    constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), expected.as_bytes())\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t/// Validate a CSRF token against the expected session token.\n274\t/// Returns Ok(()) if the token matches, or a CsrfMismatch error.\n275\tpub fn validate_csrf_token(provided: &str, expected: &str) -> Result<(), MiroirCode> {\n276\t    if constant_time_csrf_compare(provided, expected) {\n277\t        Ok(())\n278\t    } else {\n279\t        Err(MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch)\n280\t    }\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n284\t// Origin validation (plan §9)\n285\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n286\t\n287\t/// Result of origin validation.\n288\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n289\tpub enum OriginVerdict {\n290\t    /// Origin is allowed.\n291\t    Allowed,\n292\t    /// Origin is not in the allowed list.\n293\t    Forbidden,\n294\t    /// No Origin/Referer header present (for same-origin requests).\n295\t    Missing,\n296\t}\n297\t\n298\t/// Validate the Origin header against the allowed origins list.\n299\t/// Handles special \"same-origin\" value by comparing against the Host header.\n300\tpub fn validate_origin(\n301\t    headers: &HeaderMap,\n302\t    allowed_origins: &[String],\n303\t    is_same_origin_by_default: bool,\n304\t) -> OriginVerdict {\n305\t    // Try Origin header first (preferred for POST/DELETE/PUT)\n306\t    let origin = headers.get(\"origin\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok());\n307\t\n308\t    // Fall back to Referer header (for navigational requests)\n309\t    let referer = origin.or_else(|| headers.get(\"referer\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()));\n310\t\n311\t    let provided_origin = match referer {\n312\t        Some(o) => o,\n313\t        None => {\n314\t            // No Origin or Referer header - for same-origin requests, this is acceptable\n315\t            return if is_same_origin_by_default {\n316\t                OriginVerdict::Missing\n317\t            } else {\n318\t                OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n319\t            };\n320\t        }\n321\t    };\n322\t\n323\t    // Strip path from Referer to get origin\n324\t    let provided_origin = if let Some(ref_hdr) = headers.get(\"referer\") {\n325\t        if let Ok(ref_val) = ref_hdr.to_str() {\n326\t            // Find the first '/' after \"https://\" (skip the first 8 chars: \"https://\")\n327\t            if let Some(idx) = ref_val.chars().enumerate().skip(8).find(|(_, c)| *c == '/').map(|(i, _)| i) {\n328\t                &ref_val[..idx]\n329\t            } else {\n330\t                ref_val\n331\t            }\n332\t        } else {\n333\t            provided_origin\n334\t        }\n335\t    } else {\n336\t        provided_origin\n337\t    };\n338\t\n339\t    // Check against allowed origins\n340\t    for allowed in allowed_origins {\n341\t        // Special \"same-origin\" value - compare against Host header\n342\t        if allowed == \"same-origin\" {\n343\t            if let Some(host) = headers.get(\"host\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()) {\n344\t                // Construct origin from scheme (https) and host\n345\t                let same_origin = format!(\"https://{}\", host);\n346\t                if provided_origin == same_origin || provided_origin == host {\n347\t                    return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n348\t                }\n349\t            }\n350\t        } else if allowed == \"*\" {\n351\t            // Wildcard allows any origin\n352\t            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n353\t        } else if provided_origin == allowed {\n354\t            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n355\t        }\n356\t    }\n357\t\n358\t    OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n359\t}\n360\t\n361\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n362\t// CSP header builder (plan §9)\n363\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n364\t\n365\t/// Build a CSP header value by merging base template with overrides.\n366\t/// Overrides are merged additively - they never replace the base template.\n367\tpub fn build_csp_header(\n368\t    base_template: &str,\n369\t    overrides: &miroir_core::config::CspOverridesConfig,\n370\t) -> String {\n371\t    let mut directives: Vec<(String, Vec<String>)> = base_template\n372\t        .split(';')\n373\t        .map(|s| s.trim())\n374\t        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())\n375\t        .map(|directive| {\n376\t            let parts: Vec<&str> = directive.splitn(2, ' ').collect();\n377\t            if parts.len() == 2 {\n378\t                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![parts[1].to_string()])\n379\t            } else {\n380\t                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![])\n381\t            }\n382\t        })\n383\t        .collect();\n384\t\n385\t    // Helper to merge overrides into a directive\n386\t    let merge_into = |directives: &mut Vec<(String, Vec<String>)>,\n387\t                      name: &str,\n388\t                      values: &[String]| {\n389\t        if values.is_empty() {\n390\t            return;\n391\t        }\n392\t        let name_lower = name.to_lowercase();\n393\t        if let Some(entry) = directives.iter_mut().find(|(n, _)| n == &name_lower) {\n394\t            // Append to existing directive\n395\t            entry.1.extend(values.iter().cloned());\n396\t            entry.1.dedup(); // Remove duplicates\n397\t        } else {\n398\t            // Add new directive\n399\t            directives.push((name_lower, values.to_vec()));\n400\t        }\n401\t    };\n402\t\n403\t    // Merge each override category\n404\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"script-src\", &overrides.script_src);\n405\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"img-src\", &overrides.img_src);\n406\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"connect-src\", &overrides.connect_src);\n407\t\n408\t    // Rebuild CSP string\n409\t    directives\n410\t        .into_iter()\n411\t        .map(|(name, values)| {\n412\t            if values.is_empty() {\n413\t                name\n414\t            } else {\n415\t                format!(\"{} {}\", name, values.join(\" \"))\n416\t            }\n417\t        })\n418\t        .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n419\t        .join(\"; \")\n420\t}\n421\t\n422\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n423\t// Dispatch verdict\n424\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n425\t\n426\t/// Result of the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n427\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n428\tpub enum AuthVerdict {\n429\t    /// Request is dispatch-exempt (rule 0); handler decides auth.\n430\t    Exempt,\n431\t    /// Authenticated with the given token kind.\n432\t    Authenticated(TokenKind),\n433\t    /// Bearer token looked like a JWT but failed validation (rule 1).\n434\t    JwtInvalid,\n435\t    /// JWT was signature-valid but scope was insufficient (rule 1).\n436\t    JwtScopeDenied,\n437\t    /// No matching key / missing Authorization (rule 4).\n438\t    InvalidAuth,\n439\t}\n440\t\n441\t/// Which key or token type satisfied authentication.\n442\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n443\tpub enum TokenKind {\n444\t    MasterKey,\n445\t    AdminKey,\n446\t    /// JWT validated against primary or previous secret.\n447\t    Jwt,\n448\t    /// Admin session cookie — sealed session ID validated against task store.\n449\t    AdminSession,\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\timpl AuthVerdict {\n453\t    pub fn is_allowed(&self) -> bool {\n454\t        matches!(self, AuthVerdict::Exempt | AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_))\n455\t    }\n456\t}\n457\t\n458\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n459\t// Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt check\n460\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n461\t\n462\t/// Returns true when `(method, path)` is in the exhaustive dispatch-exempt list\n463\t/// (plan §5 rule 5). Exempt endpoints run their handler directly; rules 1–4\n464\t/// are never consulted.\n465\tpub fn is_dispatch_exempt(method: &Method, path: &str) -> bool {\n466\t    // `GET /health` — unauthenticated liveness probe (Meilisearch-compatible)\n467\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/health\" {\n468\t        return true;\n469\t    }\n470\t\n471\t    // `GET /version` — unauthenticated version endpoint (Meilisearch-compatible)\n472\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/version\" {\n473\t        return true;\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    // `GET /_miroir/ready` — unauthenticated readiness probe (plan §10)\n477\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/_miroir/ready\" {\n478\t        return true;\n479\t    }\n480\t\n481\t    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch\n482\t    if method == Method::GET {\n483\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/\") {\n484\t            // Must have at least one path segment after the prefix\n485\t            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains(\"//\");\n486\t        }\n487\t    }\n488\t\n489\t    // `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body\n490\t    if method == Method::POST && path == \"/_miroir/admin/login\" {\n491\t        return true;\n492\t    }\n493\t\n494\t    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per search_ui.auth.mode\n495\t    if method == Method::GET {\n496\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/\") {\n497\t            let segments: Vec<&str> = rest.split('/').collect();\n498\t            if segments.len() == 2 && segments[1] == \"session\" && !segments[0].is_empty() {\n499\t                return true;\n500\t            }\n501\t        }\n502\t    }\n503\t\n504\t    // `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA entry point\n505\t    if method == Method::GET {\n506\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/ui/search/\") {\n507\t            // Single non-empty segment (the index name)\n508\t            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains('/');\n509\t        }\n510\t    }\n511\t\n512\t    false\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n516\t// Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe\n517\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n518\t\n519\t/// Returns true if `token` has the structural shape of a JWT (three\n520\t/// dot-separated base64url segments).\n521\tpub fn probe_jwt_shape(token: &str) -> bool {\n522\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n523\t    if parts.len() != 3 {\n524\t        return false;\n525\t    }\n526\t    // Each segment should be non-empty and look like base64url\n527\t    parts.iter().all(|s| {\n528\t        !s.is_empty()\n529\t            && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '-' || c == '_' || c == '=')\n530\t    })\n531\t}\n532\t\n533\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n534\t// Helper — admin path check\n535\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n536\t\n537\t/// Returns true if `path` starts with `/_miroir/` (admin surface).\n538\tpub fn is_admin_path(path: &str) -> bool {\n539\t    path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\")\n540\t}\n541\t\n542\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n543\t// Constant-time opaque key comparison\n544\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n545\t\n546\t/// Constant-time comparison of an opaque token against an expected key.\n547\t/// Prevents timing side-channels on secret key values.\n548\tpub fn constant_time_compare(token: &[u8], expected: &[u8]) -> bool {\n549\t    token.ct_eq(expected).into()\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n553\t// Core dispatch — rules 0–4\n554\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n555\t\n556\t/// Execute the full bearer-token dispatch chain for a request.\n557\t///\n558\t/// `bearer_token` is the raw value after stripping `\"Bearer \"` from the\n559\t/// `Authorization` header (may be `None` if the header is absent).\n560\tpub fn dispatch_bearer(\n561\t    method: &Method,\n562\t    path: &str,\n563\t    bearer_token: Option<&str>,\n564\t    state: &AuthState,\n565\t) -> AuthVerdict {\n566\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt endpoints skip all auth checks\n567\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(method, path) {\n568\t        return AuthVerdict::Exempt;\n569\t    }\n570\t\n571\t    let token = match bearer_token {\n572\t        Some(t) => t,\n573\t        None => return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth, // Rule 4 — missing auth\n574\t    };\n575\t\n576\t    // Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe, then full validation\n577\t    if probe_jwt_shape(token) {\n578\t        match state.validate_jwt(token) {\n579\t            Ok(_claims) => return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt),\n580\t            Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty) => {\n581\t                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n582\t            }\n583\t            Err(_) => {\n584\t                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n585\t            }\n586\t        }\n587\t    }\n588\t\n589\t    // Rule 2 — admin-path opaque-token match\n590\t    if is_admin_path(path) {\n591\t        if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n592\t            return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::AdminKey);\n593\t        }\n594\t        return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth; // Rule 4\n595\t    }\n596\t\n597\t    // Rule 3 — master-key match (non-admin paths)\n598\t    if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.master_key.as_bytes()) {\n599\t        return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::MasterKey);\n600\t    }\n601\t\n602\t    // Rule 4 — mismatch\n603\t    AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n607\t// X-Admin-Key short-circuit\n608\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n609\t\n610\t/// Check the `X-Admin-Key` header for admin endpoints.\n611\t/// Returns `true` if the header is present and matches `admin_key`.\n612\t/// Evaluated independently of the bearer chain — short-circuits directly.\n613\tpub fn check_x_admin_key(headers: &HeaderMap, admin_key: &[u8]) -> bool {\n614\t    match headers.get(\"X-Admin-Key\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()) {\n615\t        Some(key) => constant_time_compare(key.as_bytes(), admin_key),\n616\t        None => false,\n617\t    }\n618\t}\n619\t\n620\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n621\t// Axum middleware\n622\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n623\t\n624\t/// Extract the bearer token from `Authorization: Bearer <value>`.\n625\tfn extract_bearer(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<&str> {\n626\t    let auth = headers.get(\"authorization\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n627\t    auth.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n628\t}\n629\t\n630\t/// Extract the sealed admin session cookie value from the Cookie header.\n631\tpub fn extract_admin_session_cookie(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n632\t    let cookie_header = headers.get(\"cookie\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n633\t    for pair in cookie_header.split(';') {\n634\t        let pair = pair.trim();\n635\t        if let Some(value) = pair.strip_prefix(&format!(\"{}=\", admin_session::COOKIE_NAME)) {\n636\t            return Some(value.to_string());\n637\t        }\n638\t    }\n639\t    None\n640\t}\n641\t\n642\t/// Unseal an admin session cookie, returning the session ID.\n643\tpub fn unseal_admin_cookie(cookie_value: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<String, admin_session::SealError> {\n644\t    admin_session::unseal_session(cookie_value, key)\n645\t}\n646\t\n647\t/// Axum middleware implementing the bearer-token dispatch chain (plan §5).\n648\tpub async fn auth_middleware(\n649\t    State(state): State<AuthState>,\n650\t    req: Request,\n651\t    next: Next,\n652\t) -> Response {\n653\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n654\t    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n655\t\n656\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt: skip everything, handler decides auth\n657\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n658\t        return next.run(req).await;\n659\t    }\n660\t\n661\t    // X-Admin-Key short-circuit for admin endpoints\n662\t    if is_admin_path(&path) && check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n663\t        return next.run(req).await;\n664\t    }\n665\t\n666\t    // Admin session cookie check for admin endpoints (plan §9, §13.19).\n667\t    // If a sealed admin session cookie is present, unseal it and authenticate.\n668\t    // Revoked sessions are rejected immediately.\n669\t    if is_admin_path(&path) {\n670\t        if let Some(cookie_value) = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()) {\n671\t            match unseal_admin_cookie(&cookie_value, &state.seal_key) {\n672\t                Ok(session_id) => {\n673\t                    // Check revocation cache (populated on logout + Pub/Sub).\n674\t                    if state.revoked_sessions.contains_key(&session_id) {\n675\t                        return MeilisearchError::new(\n676\t                            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n677\t                            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n678\t                        )\n679\t                        .into_response();\n680\t                    }\n681\t                    let mut req = req;\n682\t                    req.extensions_mut().insert(AdminSessionId(session_id));\n683\t                    return next.run(req).await;\n684\t                }\n685\t                Err(e) => {\n686\t                    // Cookie tampering or wrong seal key (e.g. cross-pod key\n687\t                    // mismatch in HA).  Log a warning so operators can diagnose\n688\t                    // ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY divergence across pods.\n689\t                    tracing::warn!(\n690\t                        path = %path,\n691\t                        error = %e,\n692\t                        \"admin session cookie unseal failed — tampered cookie or cross-pod key mismatch\"\n693\t                    );\n694\t                }\n695\t            }\n696\t        }\n697\t    }\n698\t\n699\t    // Extract bearer token\n700\t    let bearer = extract_bearer(req.headers());\n701\t\n702\t    // Run the dispatch chain\n703\t    let verdict = dispatch_bearer(&method, &path, bearer, &state);\n704\t\n705\t    match verdict {\n706\t        AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_) | AuthVerdict::Exempt => next.run(req).await,\n707\t        AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid => MeilisearchError::new(\n708\t            MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n709\t            \"The provided JWT is invalid or expired.\",\n710\t        )\n711\t        .into_response(),\n712\t        AuthVerdict::JwtScopeDenied => MeilisearchError::new(\n713\t            MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n714\t            \"The provided JWT does not grant access to this resource.\",\n715\t        )\n716\t        .into_response(),\n717\t        AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth => MeilisearchError::new(\n718\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n719\t            \"The provided authorization is invalid.\",\n720\t        )\n721\t        .into_response(),\n722\t    }\n723\t}\n724\t\n725\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n726\t// CSRF validation middleware (plan §9)\n727\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n728\t\n729\t/// CSRF middleware that validates `X-CSRF-Token` on state-changing requests.\n730\t///\n731\t/// Bypasses CSRF check when:\n732\t/// - Request is authenticated via Bearer token (not admin session cookie)\n733\t/// - X-Admin-Key header is present\n734\t/// - Request method is safe (GET, HEAD, OPTIONS)\n735\t/// - Path is dispatch-exempt\n736\t///\n737\t/// For admin session cookie auth, requires `X-CSRF-Token` header to match\n738\t/// the token stored in the session.\n739\tpub async fn csrf_middleware(\n740\t    State(state): State<CsrfState>,\n741\t    req: Request,\n742\t    next: Next,\n743\t) -> Response {\n744\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n745\t    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n746\t\n747\t    // Skip CSRF for safe methods\n748\t    if matches!(method, Method::GET | Method::HEAD | Method::OPTIONS) {\n749\t        return next.run(req).await;\n750\t    }\n751\t\n752\t    // Skip CSRF for non-admin paths\n753\t    if !is_admin_path(&path) {\n754\t        return next.run(req).await;\n755\t    }\n756\t\n757\t    // Skip CSRF for dispatch-exempt endpoints\n758\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n759\t        return next.run(req).await;\n760\t    }\n761\t\n762\t    // Skip CSRF if X-Admin-Key is present (bypasses CSRF)\n763\t    if check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.auth.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n764\t        return next.run(req).await;\n765\t    }\n766\t\n767\t    // Check if authenticated via admin session cookie\n768\t    let has_session_cookie = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()).is_some();\n769\t    let has_bearer_token = extract_bearer(req.headers()).is_some();\n770\t\n771\t    // CSRF only applies to session-cookie auth, not bearer tokens\n772\t    if !has_session_cookie || has_bearer_token {\n773\t        return next.run(req).await;\n774\t    }\n775\t\n776\t    // Extract CSRF token from header\n777\t    let csrf_token = match extract_csrf_token(req.headers()) {\n778\t        Some(token) => token,\n779\t        None => {\n780\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n781\t                MiroirCode::MissingCsrf,\n782\t                \"CSRF token is required for state-changing requests.\",\n783\t            )\n784\t            .into_response();\n785\t        }\n786\t    };\n787\t\n788\t    // Get session ID from extensions (set by auth_middleware)\n789\t    let session_id = match req.extensions().get::<AdminSessionId>() {\n790\t        Some(id) => id.0.clone(),\n791\t        None => {\n792\t            // Session cookie was present but auth_middleware didn't set AdminSessionId\n793\t            // This means the session was invalid/expired/revoked\n794\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n795\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n796\t                \"Admin session is invalid or expired.\",\n797\t            )\n798\t            .into_response();\n799\t        }\n800\t    };\n801\t\n802\t    // Validate CSRF token against session\n803\t    let Some(redis_store) = state.redis_store.as_ref() else {\n804\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n805\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n806\t            \"Admin sessions require Redis task store.\",\n807\t        )\n808\t        .into_response();\n809\t    };\n810\t\n811\t    let session = match redis_store.get_admin_session(&session_id) {\n812\t        Ok(Some(s)) => s,\n813\t        Ok(None) => {\n814\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n815\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n816\t                \"Admin session not found.\",\n817\t            )\n818\t            .into_response();\n819\t        }\n820\t        Err(e) => {\n821\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, session_prefix = &session_id[..session_id.len().min(8)], \"failed to get admin session for CSRF validation\");\n822\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n823\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n824\t                \"Failed to validate session.\",\n825\t            )\n826\t            .into_response();\n827\t        }\n828\t    };\n829\t\n830\t    // Check if revoked\n831\t    if session.revoked {\n832\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n833\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n834\t            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n835\t        )\n836\t        .into_response();\n837\t    }\n838\t\n839\t    // Check expiration\n840\t    let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n841\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n842\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n843\t        .as_secs() as i64;\n844\t    if session.expires_at < now {\n845\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n846\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n847\t            \"Admin session has expired.\",\n848\t        )\n849\t        .into_response();\n850\t    }\n851\t\n852\t    // Constant-time compare CSRF tokens\n853\t    if !constant_time_csrf_compare(&csrf_token, &session.csrf_token) {\n854\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n855\t            MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch,\n856\t            \"CSRF token does not match the session token.\",\n857\t        )\n858\t        .into_response();\n859\t    }\n860\t\n861\t    next.run(req).await\n862\t}\n863\t\n864\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n865\t// Rate-limit hook types (Phase 2 in-memory stub, Phase 6 multi-pod)\n866\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n867\t\n868\t/// Rate-limit bucket key types wired into the dispatch chain.\n869\t/// Phase 2 keeps these as in-memory counters; Phase 6 will back them\n870\t/// with the task store (Redis/SQLite).\n871\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n872\tpub enum RateLimitBucket {\n873\t    /// `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`\n874\t    AdminLogin(String),\n875\t    /// `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>`\n876\t    SearchUi(String),\n877\t}\n878\t\n879\t/// In-memory rate limiter (Phase 2 stub). Always returns `Ok(())` — actual\n880\t/// enforcement is deferred to Phase 6 multi-pod. The hook is wired here so\n881\t/// handlers can call `limiter.check()` without cfg-gating.\n882\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n883\tpub struct RateLimiter;\n884\t\n885\timpl RateLimiter {\n886\t    pub fn check(&self, _bucket: &RateLimitBucket) -> Result<(), ()> {\n887\t        Ok(()) // Phase 2: always allow\n888\t    }\n889\t}\n890\t\n891\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n892\t// Helpers\n893\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n894\t\n895\tfn epoch_seconds() -> u64 {\n896\t    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n897\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n898\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n899\t        .as_secs()\n900\t}\n901\t\n902\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n903\t// Tests\n904\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n905\t\n906\t#[cfg(test)]\n907\tmod tests {\n908\t    use super::*;\n909\t\n910\t    fn test_key() -> SealKey {\n911\t        SealKey::from_bytes([42u8; 32])\n912\t    }\n913\t\n914\t    fn test_state() -> AuthState {\n915\t        AuthState {\n916\t            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n917\t            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n918\t            jwt_primary: None,\n919\t            jwt_previous: None,\n920\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n921\t            revoked_sessions: ).unwrap(),\n925\t        }\n926\t    }\n927\t\n928\t    fn test_state_with_jwt() -> AuthState {\n929\t        AuthState {\n930\t            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n931\t            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n932\t            jwt_primary: Some(\"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\".to_string()),\n933\t            jwt_previous: None,\n934\t            seal_key: {\n944\t            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n945\t            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n946\t            jwt_primary:       ).unwrap(),\n953\t        }\n954\t    }\n955\t\n956\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n957\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt tests\n958\t    //   }\n1147\t\n1148\t    #[test]\n1149\t    fn jwt_shape_probe_rejects_empty() {\n1150\t        assert!(!probe_jwt_shape(\"\"));\n1151\t    }\n1152\t\n1153\t    #[test]\n1154\t    fn assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid);\n1182\t    }\n1183\t\n1184\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1185\t    // JWT signing and validation — primary secret\n1186\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1187\t\n1188\t    #[test]\n1189\t    fn sign_and_validate_primary_jwt() {\n1190\t        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n1191\t        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n1192\t\n1193\t        let claims = state.validate_jwt(&token).unwrap();\n1194\t        assert_eq!(claims.sub, \"user1\");\n1195\t        assert_eq!(claims.idx, \"products\");\n1196\t        assert_eq!(claims.scope, \"search\");\n1197\t    }\n1198\t\n1199\t    #[test]\n1200\t    fn signed_jwt_authenticates_via_dispatch() {\n1201\t        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n1202\t        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n1203\t\n1204\t        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n1205\t            &Method::GET,\n1206\t            \"/indexes/products\",\n1207\t            Some(&token),\n1208\t            &state,\n1209\t        );\n1210\t        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt));\n1211\t    }\n1212\t\n1213\t    #[test]\n1214\t    fn expired_jwt_returns_jwt_invalid() {\n1215\t        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n1216\t        let now = epoch_seconds();\n1217\t        let claims = JwtClaims {\n1218\t            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n1219\t            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n1220\t            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n1221\t            iat: now - 3600,\n1222\t            exp: now - 100, // expired well beyond 30s leeway\n1223\t        };\n1224\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n1225\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n1226\t            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n1227\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n1228\t        };\n1229\t        let token = jwt_encode(\n1230\t            &header,\n1231\t            &claims,\n1232\t            state.jwt_primary.as_ref().unwrap().as_bytes(),\n1233\t        )\n1234\t        .unwrap();\n1235\t\n1236\t        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n1237\t        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::Expired));\n1238\t    }\n1239\t\n1240\t    #[test]\n1241\t    fn tampered_signature_returns_invalid_signature() {\n1242\t        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n1243\t        let mut token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n1244\t        // Tamper with the signature\n1245\t        let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n1246\t        token = format!(\"{}.{}.tampered_sig\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n1247\t\n1248\t        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n1249\t        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature));\n1250\t    }\n1251\t\n1252\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1253\t    // JWT dual-secret rotation validation\n1254\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1255\t\n1256\t    #[test]\n1257\t    fn rotation_old_token_validates_via_previous_secret() {\n1258\t        let primary = \"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\";\n1259\t        let previous = \"test-secret-previous-key-32byte\";\n1260\t\n1261\t        // Sign token with the previous secret\n1262\t        let now = epoch_seconds();\n1263\t        let claims = JwtClaims {\n1264\t            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n1265\t            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n1266\t            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n1267\t            iat: now,\n1268\t            exp: now + 900,\n1269\t        };\n1270\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n1271\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n1272\t            kid: KID_PREVIOUS.to_string(),\n1273\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n1274\t        };\n1275\t        let old_token = jwt_encode(&header, &claims, previous.as_bytes()).unwrap();\n1276\t\n1277\t        // Simulate rotation — new primary, old primary as previous\n1278\t        let state = AuthState {\n1279\t            master_key:       seal_key: test_key(),\n1284\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n1285\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n1286\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n1287\t            ).unwrap(),\n1288\t        };\n1289\t\n1290\t        // Old token should still validate via previous secret\n1291\t assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt));\n1302\t    }\n1303\t\n1304\t    #[test]\n1305\t    fn rotation_new_token_validates_via_primary_secret() {\n1306\t        let state = Bearer-token dispatch per plan §5 rules 0–5.\n//!\n//! Three token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously:\n//! the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them\n//! deterministically in the order specified by §5.\n//!\n//! JWT signing-secret rotation (plan §9):\n//! - Primary secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET`) signs new tokens; `kid` header identifies it.\n//! - Optional previous secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS`) is present only during\n//!   the rotation overlap window. Validation accepts either secret.\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{FromRef, Request, State},\n    http::{HeaderMap, Method},\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::{IntoResponse, Response},\n};\nuse base64::{engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD, Engine as _};\nuse dashmap::DashMap;\nuse hmac::{Hmac, Mac};\nuse miroir_core::{task_store::TaskStore, MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\nuse prometheus::Counter;\nuse rand::RngCore;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse sha2::Sha256;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse subtle::ConstantTimeEq;\n\nuse crate::admin_session::{self, SealKey};\n\ntype HmacSha256 = Hmac<Sha256>;\n\n/// Extension carried in the request after successful cookie unseal.\n/// Handlers extract this to look up the session in the task store.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct AdminSessionId(pub String);\n\n/// State for CSRF middleware, combining AuthState with task store access.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct CsrfState {\n    pub auth: AuthState,\n    pub redis_store: Option<miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore>,\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// JWT claims (plan §13.21)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Claims embedded in a search UI JWT session token.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JwtClaims {\n    /// Subject — user identifier or \"anonymous\".\n    pub sub: String,\n    /// Index this token grants access to.\n    pub idx: String,\n    /// Granted scope (e.g. \"search\").\n    pub scope: String,\n    /// Issued-at timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n    pub iat: u64,\n    /// Expiration timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n    pub exp: u64,\n}\n\n/// Key ID embedded in the JWT header to identify which secret signed it.\nconst KID_PRIMARY: &str = \"primary\";\nconst KID_PREVIOUS: &str = \"previous\";\n\n/// JWT header (always HS256).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\nstruct JwtHeader {\n    alg: String,\n    kid: String,\n    typ: String,\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Minimal HS256 JWT encode / decode (no external JWT crate needed)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Encode and sign a JWT with the given secret.\nfn jwt_encode(header: &JwtHeader, claims: &JwtClaims, secret: &[u8]) -> Result<String, String> {\n    let header_json = serde_json::to_string(header).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n    let claims_json = serde_json::to_string(claims).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n\n    let header_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(header_json.as_bytes());\n    let payload_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(claims_json.as_bytes());\n\n    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64);\n\n    let mut mac =\n        HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).map_err(|e| format!(\"HMAC init: {}\", e))?;\n    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n    let sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n    let sig_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(sig);\n\n    Ok(format!(\"{}.{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64, sig_b64))\n}\n\n/// Decode and verify a JWT with the given secret. Returns (header, claims).\nfn jwt_decode(\n    token: &str,\n    secret: &[u8],\n) -> Result<(JwtHeader, JwtClaims), JwtValidationError> {\n    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n    if parts.len() != 3 {\n        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n    }\n\n    let header_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n        .decode(parts[0])\n        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n    let header: JwtHeader =\n        serde_json::from_slice(&header_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n\n    if header.alg != \"HS256\" {\n        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n    }\n\n    // Verify signature\n    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n    let mut mac = HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).unwrap();\n    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n    let expected_sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n\n    let actual_sig = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n        .decode(parts[2])\n        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)?;\n\n    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n    let sig_valid: bool = actual_sig.ct_eq(&expected_sig).into();\n    if !sig_valid {\n        return Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature);\n    }\n\n    // Decode claims\n    let claims_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n        .decode(parts[1])\n        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n    let claims: JwtClaims =\n        serde_json::from_slice(&claims_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n\n    // Check expiration with 30s leeway\n    let now = epoch_seconds();\n    if claims.exp + 30 < now {\n        return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired);\n    }\n\n    Ok((header, claims))\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Auth state (shared via axum State)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Configuration needed by the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AuthState {\n    pub master_key: String,\n    pub admin_key: String,\n    /// HMAC secret for signing/validating search UI JWTs (primary).\n    pub jwt_primary: Option<String>,\n    /// Optional previous secret active during rotation overlap window.\n    pub jwt_previous: Option<String>,\n    /// Key for sealing/unsealing admin session cookies (XChaCha20-Poly1305).\n    pub seal_key: SealKey,\n    /// In-memory set of revoked admin session IDs (populated on logout, Pub/Sub).\n    pub revoked_sessions: Arc<DashMap<String, ()>>,\n    /// Counter for revoked admin sessions (miroir_admin_session_revoked_total).\n    pub admin_session_revoked_total: Counter,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for AuthState {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"AuthState\")\n            .field(\"master_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"admin_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"jwt_primary\", &self.jwt_primary.as_ref().map(|_| \"[set]\"))\n            .field(\"jwt_previous\", &self.jwt_previous.as_ref().map(|_| \"[set]\"))\n            .field(\"seal_key\", &self.seal_key)\n            .field(\"revoked_sessions\", &self.revoked_sessions.len())\n            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// JWT signing / validation helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nimpl AuthState {\n    /// Create a new signed JWT session token for the given index.\n    /// Always signs with the primary secret; `kid` header identifies it.\n    pub fn sign_jwt(&self, sub: &str, idx: &str, scope: &str, ttl_s: u64) -> Option<String> {\n        let secret = self.jwt_primary.as_ref()?;\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: sub.to_string(),\n            idx: idx.to_string(),\n            scope: scope.to_string(),\n            iat: now,\n            exp: now + ttl_s,\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        jwt_encode(&header, &claims, secret.as_bytes()).ok()\n    }\n\n    /// Validate a JWT string against either the primary or previous secret.\n    /// Returns the parsed claims if validation succeeds, or an error.\n    pub fn validate_jwt(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, JwtValidationError> {\n        // Try primary secret first\n        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_primary {\n            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n                _ => {} // signature mismatch — try previous\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Try previous secret (rotation overlap window)\n        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_previous {\n            if secret.is_empty() {\n                return Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty);\n            }\n            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n                _ => {} // signature mismatch\n            }\n        }\n\n        Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Errors returned by JWT validation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum JwtValidationError {\n    /// Token structure is invalid or alg is not HS256.\n    Malformed,\n    /// HMAC signature did not match any loaded secret.\n    InvalidSignature,\n    /// Token has expired.\n    Expired,\n    /// `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS` is set to the empty string (leak response).\n    PreviousSecretEmpty,\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// CSRF token generation (plan §9)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Generate a cryptographically random CSRF token.\n/// Returns a URL-safe base64-encoded 32-byte token.\npub fn generate_csrf_token() -> String {\n    let mut bytes = [0u8; 32];\n    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n    URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(&bytes)\n}\n\n/// Extract the CSRF token from the `X-CSRF-Token` header.\npub fn extract_csrf_token(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n    headers.get(\"X-CSRF-Token\")?.to_str().ok().map(String::from)\n}\n\n/// Constant-time comparison of CSRF tokens.\npub fn constant_time_csrf_compare(token: &str, expected: &str) -> bool {\n    constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), expected.as_bytes())\n}\n\n/// Validate a CSRF token against the expected session token.\n/// Returns Ok(()) if the token matches, or a CsrfMismatch error.\npub fn validate_csrf_token(provided: &str, expected: &str) -> Result<(), MiroirCode> {\n    if constant_time_csrf_compare(provided, expected) {\n        Ok(())\n    } else {\n        Err(MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch)\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Origin validation (plan §9)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Result of origin validation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum OriginVerdict {\n    /// Origin is allowed.\n    Allowed,\n    /// Origin is not in the allowed list.\n    Forbidden,\n    /// No Origin/Referer header present (for same-origin requests).\n    Missing,\n}\n\n/// Validate the Origin header against the allowed origins list.\n/// Handles special \"same-origin\" value by comparing against the Host header.\npub fn validate_origin(\n    headers: &HeaderMap,\n    allowed_origins: &[String],\n    is_same_origin_by_default: bool,\n) -> OriginVerdict {\n    // Try Origin header first (preferred for POST/DELETE/PUT)\n    let origin = headers.get(\"origin\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok());\n\n    // Fall back to Referer header (for navigational requests)\n    let referer = origin.or_else(|| headers.get(\"referer\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()));\n\n    let provided_origin = match referer {\n        Some(o) => o,\n        None => {\n            // No Origin or Referer header - for same-origin requests, this is acceptable\n            return if is_same_origin_by_default {\n                OriginVerdict::Missing\n            } else {\n                OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n            };\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Strip path from Referer to get origin\n    let provided_origin = if let Some(ref_hdr) = headers.get(\"referer\") {\n        if let Ok(ref_val) = ref_hdr.to_str() {\n            // Find the first '/' after \"https://\" (skip the first 8 chars: \"https://\")\n            if let Some(idx) = ref_val.chars().enumerate().skip(8).find(|(_, c)| *c == '/').map(|(i, _)| i) {\n                &ref_val[..idx]\n            } else {\n                ref_val\n            }\n        } else {\n            provided_origin\n        }\n    } else {\n        provided_origin\n    };\n\n    // Check against allowed origins\n    for allowed in allowed_origins {\n        // Special \"same-origin\" value - compare against Host header\n        if allowed == \"same-origin\" {\n            if let Some(host) = headers.get(\"host\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()) {\n                // Construct origin from scheme (https) and host\n                let same_origin = format!(\"https://{}\", host);\n                if provided_origin == same_origin || provided_origin == host {\n                    return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n                }\n            }\n        } else if allowed == \"*\" {\n            // Wildcard allows any origin\n            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n        } else if provided_origin == allowed {\n            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n        }\n    }\n\n    OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// CSP header builder (plan §9)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Build a CSP header value by merging base template with overrides.\n/// Overrides are merged additively - they never replace the base template.\npub fn build_csp_header(\n    base_template: &str,\n    overrides: &miroir_core::config::CspOverridesConfig,\n) -> String {\n    let mut directives: Vec<(String, Vec<String>)> = base_template\n        .split(';')\n        .map(|s| s.trim())\n        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())\n        .map(|directive| {\n            let parts: Vec<&str> = directive.splitn(2, ' ').collect();\n            if parts.len() == 2 {\n                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![parts[1].to_string()])\n            } else {\n                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![])\n            }\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Helper to merge overrides into a directive\n    let merge_into = |directives: &mut Vec<(String, Vec<String>)>,\n                      name: &str,\n                      values: &[String]| {\n        if values.is_empty() {\n            return;\n        }\n        let name_lower = name.to_lowercase();\n        if let Some(entry) = directives.iter_mut().find(|(n, _)| n == &name_lower) {\n            // Append to existing directive\n            entry.1.extend(values.iter().cloned());\n            entry.1.dedup(); // Remove duplicates\n        } else {\n            // Add new directive\n            directives.push((name_lower, values.to_vec()));\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Merge each override category\n    merge_into(&mut directives, \"script-src\", &overrides.script_src);\n    merge_into(&mut directives, \"img-src\", &overrides.img_src);\n    merge_into(&mut directives, \"connect-src\", &overrides.connect_src);\n\n    // Rebuild CSP string\n    directives\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(name, values)| {\n            if values.is_empty() {\n                name\n            } else {\n                format!(\"{} {}\", name, values.join(\" \"))\n            }\n        })\n        .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n        .join(\"; \")\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Dispatch verdict\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Result of the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum AuthVerdict {\n    /// Request is dispatch-exempt (rule 0); handler decides auth.\n    Exempt,\n    /// Authenticated with the given token kind.\n    Authenticated(TokenKind),\n    /// Bearer token looked like a JWT but failed validation (rule 1).\n    JwtInvalid,\n    /// JWT was signature-valid but scope was insufficient (rule 1).\n    JwtScopeDenied,\n    /// No matching key / missing Authorization (rule 4).\n    InvalidAuth,\n}\n\n/// Which key or token type satisfied authentication.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum TokenKind {\n    MasterKey,\n    AdminKey,\n    /// JWT validated against primary or previous secret.\n    Jwt,\n    /// Admin session cookie — sealed session ID validated against task store.\n    AdminSession,\n}\n\nimpl AuthVerdict {\n    pub fn is_allowed(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, AuthVerdict::Exempt | AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_))\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt check\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Returns true when `(method, path)` is in the exhaustive dispatch-exempt list\n/// (plan §5 rule 5). Exempt endpoints run their handler directly; rules 1–4\n/// are never consulted.\npub fn is_dispatch_exempt(method: &Method, path: &str) -> bool {\n    // `GET /health` — unauthenticated liveness probe (Meilisearch-compatible)\n    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/health\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // `GET /version` — unauthenticated version endpoint (Meilisearch-compatible)\n    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/version\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // `GET /_miroir/ready` — unauthenticated readiness probe (plan §10)\n    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/_miroir/ready\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch\n    if method == Method::GET {\n        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/\") {\n            // Must have at least one path segment after the prefix\n            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains(\"//\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body\n    if method == Method::POST && path == \"/_miroir/admin/login\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per search_ui.auth.mode\n    if method == Method::GET {\n        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/\") {\n            let segments: Vec<&str> = rest.split('/').collect();\n            if segments.len() == 2 && segments[1] == \"session\" && !segments[0].is_empty() {\n                return true;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA entry point\n    if method == Method::GET {\n        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/ui/search/\") {\n            // Single non-empty segment (the index name)\n            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains('/');\n        }\n    }\n\n    false\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Returns true if `token` has the structural shape of a JWT (three\n/// dot-separated base64url segments).\npub fn probe_jwt_shape(token: &str) -> bool {\n    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n    if parts.len() != 3 {\n        return false;\n    }\n    // Each segment should be non-empty and look like base64url\n    parts.iter().all(|s| {\n        !s.is_empty()\n            && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '-' || c == '_' || c == '=')\n    })\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helper — admin path check\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Returns true if `path` starts with `/_miroir/` (admin surface).\npub fn is_admin_path(path: &str) -> bool {\n    path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\")\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Constant-time opaque key comparison\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Constant-time comparison of an opaque token against an expected key.\n/// Prevents timing side-channels on secret key values.\npub fn constant_time_compare(token: &[u8], expected: &[u8]) -> bool {\n    token.ct_eq(expected).into()\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Core dispatch — rules 0–4\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Execute the full bearer-token dispatch chain for a request.\n///\n/// `bearer_token` is the raw value after stripping `\"Bearer \"` from the\n/// `Authorization` header (may be `None` if the header is absent).\npub fn dispatch_bearer(\n    method: &Method,\n    path: &str,\n    bearer_token: Option<&str>,\n    state: &AuthState,\n) -> AuthVerdict {\n    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt endpoints skip all auth checks\n    if is_dispatch_exempt(method, path) {\n        return AuthVerdict::Exempt;\n    }\n\n    let token = match bearer_token {\n        Some(t) => t,\n        None => return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth, // Rule 4 — missing auth\n    };\n\n    // Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe, then full validation\n    if probe_jwt_shape(token) {\n        match state.validate_jwt(token) {\n            Ok(_claims) => return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt),\n            Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty) => {\n                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Rule 2 — admin-path opaque-token match\n    if is_admin_path(path) {\n        if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n            return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::AdminKey);\n        }\n        return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth; // Rule 4\n    }\n\n    // Rule 3 — master-key match (non-admin paths)\n    if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.master_key.as_bytes()) {\n        return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::MasterKey);\n    }\n\n    // Rule 4 — mismatch\n    AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// X-Admin-Key short-circuit\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Check the `X-Admin-Key` header for admin endpoints.\n/// Returns `true` if the header is present and matches `admin_key`.\n/// Evaluated independently of the bearer chain — short-circuits directly.\npub fn check_x_admin_key(headers: &HeaderMap, admin_key: &[u8]) -> bool {\n    match headers.get(\"X-Admin-Key\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()) {\n        Some(key) => constant_time_compare(key.as_bytes(), admin_key),\n        None => false,\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Axum middleware\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Extract the bearer token from `Authorization: Bearer <value>`.\nfn extract_bearer(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<&str> {\n    let auth = headers.get(\"authorization\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n    auth.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n}\n\n/// Extract the sealed admin session cookie value from the Cookie header.\npub fn extract_admin_session_cookie(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n    let cookie_header = headers.get(\"cookie\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n    for pair in cookie_header.split(';') {\n        let pair = pair.trim();\n        if let Some(value) = pair.strip_prefix(&format!(\"{}=\", admin_session::COOKIE_NAME)) {\n            return Some(value.to_string());\n        }\n    }\n    None\n}\n\n/// Unseal an admin session cookie, returning the session ID.\npub fn unseal_admin_cookie(cookie_value: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<String, admin_session::SealError> {\n    admin_session::unseal_session(cookie_value, key)\n}\n\n/// Axum middleware implementing the bearer-token dispatch chain (plan §5).\npub async fn auth_middleware(\n    State(state): State<AuthState>,\n    req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let method = req.method().clone();\n    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt: skip everything, handler decides auth\n    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // X-Admin-Key short-circuit for admin endpoints\n    if is_admin_path(&path) && check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // Admin session cookie check for admin endpoints (plan §9, §13.19).\n    // If a sealed admin session cookie is present, unseal it and authenticate.\n    // Revoked sessions are rejected immediately.\n    if is_admin_path(&path) {\n        if let Some(cookie_value) = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()) {\n            match unseal_admin_cookie(&cookie_value, &state.seal_key) {\n                Ok(session_id) => {\n                    // Check revocation cache (populated on logout + Pub/Sub).\n                    if state.revoked_sessions.contains_key(&session_id) {\n                        return MeilisearchError::new(\n                            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n                            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n                        )\n                        .into_response();\n                    }\n                    let mut req = req;\n                    req.extensions_mut().insert(AdminSessionId(session_id));\n                    return next.run(req).await;\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    // Cookie tampering or wrong seal key (e.g. cross-pod key\n                    // mismatch in HA).  Log a warning so operators can diagnose\n                    // ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY divergence across pods.\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        path = %path,\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"admin session cookie unseal failed — tampered cookie or cross-pod key mismatch\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Extract bearer token\n    let bearer = extract_bearer(req.headers());\n\n    // Run the dispatch chain\n    let verdict = dispatch_bearer(&method, &path, bearer, &state);\n\n    match verdict {\n        AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_) | AuthVerdict::Exempt => next.run(req).await,\n        AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid => MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n            \"The provided JWT is invalid or expired.\",\n        )\n        .into_response(),\n        AuthVerdict::JwtScopeDenied => MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n            \"The provided JWT does not grant access to this resource.\",\n        )\n        .into_response(),\n        AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth => MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n            \"The provided authorization is invalid.\",\n        )\n        .into_response(),\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// CSRF validation middleware (plan §9)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// CSRF middleware that validates `X-CSRF-Token` on state-changing requests.\n///\n/// Bypasses CSRF check when:\n/// - Request is authenticated via Bearer token (not admin session cookie)\n/// - X-Admin-Key header is present\n/// - Request method is safe (GET, HEAD, OPTIONS)\n/// - Path is dispatch-exempt\n///\n/// For admin session cookie auth, requires `X-CSRF-Token` header to match\n/// the token stored in the session.\npub async fn csrf_middleware(\n    State(state): State<CsrfState>,\n    req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let method = req.method().clone();\n    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    // Skip CSRF for safe methods\n    if matches!(method, Method::GET | Method::HEAD | Method::OPTIONS) {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // Skip CSRF for non-admin paths\n    if !is_admin_path(&path) {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // Skip CSRF for dispatch-exempt endpoints\n    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // Skip CSRF if X-Admin-Key is present (bypasses CSRF)\n    if check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.auth.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // Check if authenticated via admin session cookie\n    let has_session_cookie = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()).is_some();\n    let has_bearer_token = extract_bearer(req.headers()).is_some();\n\n    // CSRF only applies to session-cookie auth, not bearer tokens\n    if !has_session_cookie || has_bearer_token {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // Extract CSRF token from header\n    let csrf_token = match extract_csrf_token(req.headers()) {\n        Some(token) => token,\n        None => {\n            return MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::MissingCsrf,\n                \"CSRF token is required for state-changing requests.\",\n            )\n            .into_response();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Get session ID from extensions (set by auth_middleware)\n    let session_id = match req.extensions().get::<AdminSessionId>() {\n        Some(id) => id.0.clone(),\n        None => {\n            // Session cookie was present but auth_middleware didn't set AdminSessionId\n            // This means the session was invalid/expired/revoked\n            return MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n                \"Admin session is invalid or expired.\",\n            )\n            .into_response();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Validate CSRF token against session\n    let Some(redis_store) = state.redis_store.as_ref() else {\n        return MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n            \"Admin sessions require Redis task store.\",\n        )\n        .into_response();\n    };\n\n    let session = match redis_store.get_admin_session(&session_id) {\n        Ok(Some(s)) => s,\n        Ok(None) => {\n            return MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n                \"Admin session not found.\",\n            )\n            .into_response();\n        }\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, session_prefix = &session_id[..session_id.len().min(8)], \"failed to get admin session for CSRF validation\");\n            return MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n                \"Failed to validate session.\",\n            )\n            .into_response();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Check if revoked\n    if session.revoked {\n        return MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n        )\n        .into_response();\n    }\n\n    // Check expiration\n    let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs() as i64;\n    if session.expires_at < now {\n        return MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n            \"Admin session has expired.\",\n        )\n        .into_response();\n    }\n\n    // Constant-time compare CSRF tokens\n    if !constant_time_csrf_compare(&csrf_token, &session.csrf_token) {\n        return MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch,\n            \"CSRF token does not match the session token.\",\n        )\n        .into_response();\n    }\n\n    next.run(req).await\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Rate-limit hook types (Phase 2 in-memory stub, Phase 6 multi-pod)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Rate-limit bucket key types wired into the dispatch chain.\n/// Phase 2 keeps these as in-memory counters; Phase 6 will back them\n/// with the task store (Redis/SQLite).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum RateLimitBucket {\n    /// `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`\n    AdminLogin(String),\n    /// `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>`\n    SearchUi(String),\n}\n\n/// In-memory rate limiter (Phase 2 stub). Always returns `Ok(())` — actual\n/// enforcement is deferred to Phase 6 multi-pod. The hook is wired here so\n/// handlers can call `limiter.check()` without cfg-gating.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct RateLimiter;\n\nimpl RateLimiter {\n    pub fn check(&self, _bucket: &RateLimitBucket) -> Result<(), ()> {\n        Ok(()) // Phase 2: always allow\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfn epoch_seconds() -> u64 {\n    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    fn test_key() -> SealKey {\n        SealKey::from_bytes([42u8; 32])\n    }\n\n    fn test_state() -> AuthState {\n        AuthState {\n            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: None,\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn test_state_with_jwt() -> AuthState {\n        AuthState {\n            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(\"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\".to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn test_state_with_dual_jwt() -> AuthState {\n        AuthState {\n            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(\"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\".to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(\"test-secret-previous-key-32byte\".to_string()),\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt tests\n    //
