-----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // JWT signing and validation — primary secret\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn sign_and_validate_primary_jwt() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n\n        let claims = state.validate_jwt(&token).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claims.sub, \"user1\");\n        assert_eq!(claims.idx, \"products\");\n        assert_eq!(claims.scope, \"search\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn signed_jwt_authenticates_via_dispatch() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/indexes/products\",\n            Some(&token),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn expired_jwt_returns_jwt_invalid() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: now - 3600,\n            exp: now - 100, // expired well beyond 30s leeway\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let token = jwt_encode(\n            &header,\n            &claims,\n            state.jwt_primary.as_ref().unwrap().as_bytes(),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::Expired));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn tampered_signature_returns_invalid_signature() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let mut token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        // Tamper with the signature\n        let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n        token = format!(\"{}.{}.tampered_sig\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature));\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // JWT dual-secret rotation validation\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_old_token_validates_via_previous_secret() {\n        let primary = \"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\";\n        let previous = \"test-secret-previous-key-32byte\";\n\n        // Sign token with the previous secret\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: now,\n            exp: now + 900,\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PREVIOUS.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let old_token = jwt_encode(&header, &claims, previous.as_bytes()).unwrap();\n\n        // Simulate rotation — new primary, old primary as previous\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(primary.to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(previous.to_string()),\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n\n        // Old token should still validate via previous secret\n        let validated = state.validate_jwt(&old_token).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(validated.sub, \"user1\");\n\n        // And dispatch should authenticate it\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/indexes/products\",\n            Some(&old_token),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_new_token_validates_via_primary_secret() {\n        let state = test_state_with_dual_jwt();\n        let new_token = state.sign_jwt(\"user2\", \"orders\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n\n        let validated = state.validate_jwt(&new_token).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(validated.sub, \"user2\");\n        assert_eq!(validated.idx, \"orders\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_wrong_secret_returns_invalid_signature() {\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(\"correct-secret-key-32bytes-long!!!\".to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(\"previous-secret-key-32bytes-long!!\".to_string()),\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n\n        // Token signed with a completely different secret\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: now,\n            exp: now + 900,\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let token = jwt_encode(\n            &header,\n            &claims,\n            \"wrong-secret-key-32bytes-long!!!!\".as_bytes(),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn leak_response_empty_previous_rejects_old_tokens() {\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(\"new-primary-secret-key-32bytes!!\".to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(String::new()), // empty = leak response\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(\"eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJ0ZXN0In0.fake\");\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_after_step5_steady_state_previous_removed() {\n        let primary = \"final-primary-secret-key-32bytes\";\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(primary.to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n\n        // Tokens signed with current primary work\n        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        assert!(state.validate_jwt(&token).is_ok());\n\n        // Old tokens signed with now-removed previous fail\n        let old_claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: epoch_seconds() - 100,\n            exp: epoch_seconds() + 800,\n        };\n        let old_header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PREVIOUS.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let old_token = jwt_encode(\n            &old_header,\n            &old_claims,\n            \"old-previous-secret-now-removed\".as_bytes(),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n        assert!(state.validate_jwt(&old_token).is_err());\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // End-to-end rotation scenario\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn full_rotation_e2e() {\n        let secret_v1 = \"version-1-secret-key-32bytes-long!\";\n        let secret_v2 = \"version-2-secret-key-32bytes-long!\";\n\n        // Pre-rotation: only v1\n        let pre = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v1.into()),\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n        let token_v1 = pre.sign_jwt(\"alice\", \"idx\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        assert!(pre.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_ok());\n\n        // During rotation: v2 primary, v1 previous\n        let during = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v2.into()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(secret_v1.into()),\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n        // Old token still validates\n        assert!(during.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_ok());\n        // New tokens work too\n        let token_v2 = during.sign_jwt(\"bob\", \"idx\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        assert!(during.validate_jwt(&token_v2).is_ok());\n\n        // Post-rotation: only v2\n        let post = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v2.into()),\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n        // New token still works\n        assert!(post.validate_jwt(&token_v2).is_ok());\n        // Old token is rejected (signed with v1, no previous loaded)\n        assert!(post.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_err());\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Rule 2 — admin-path opaque-token match (admin_key only)\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn admin_path_matches_admin_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/_miroir/some/endpoint\",\n            Some(\"admin-key-456\"),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::AdminKey));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn admin_path_rejects_master_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/_miroir/some/endpoint\",\n            Some(\"master-key-123\"),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn admin_path_rejects_wrong_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/_miroir/some/endpoint\",\n            Some(\"wrong-key\"),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn admin_path_rejects_missing_auth() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/_miroir/some/endpoint\",\n            None,\n            &state,\n        );\n assert!(!constant_time_compare(b\"hello\", b\"world\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn constant_time_eq_different_lengths() {\n        assert!(!constant_time_compare(b\"short\", measurable delta between\n    /// \"all bytes wrong\" and \"one byte wrong\" comparisons at the same length.\n    #[test]\n    fn constant_time_no_timing_leak() {\n        use std::time::Instant;\n\n        let expected = b\"admin-key-456\";\n        let all_wrong = b\"xxxxxxxxxxxxx\";\n        let one_wrong = b\"admin-key-457\";\n\n        let iterations = 100_000u64;\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        for _ in 0..iterations {\n            let _ = constant_time_compare(all_wrong, expected);\n        }\n        let all_wrong_duration = start.elapsed();\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        for _ in 0..iterations {\n            let _ = constant_time_compare(one_wrong, expected);\n        }\n        let one_wrong_duration = start.elapsed();\n\n        let ratio = all_wrong_duration.as_secs_f64() / one_wrong_duration.as_secs_f64();\n        assert!(\n            ratio > 0.5 && ratio < 2.0,\n            \"Timing ratio {} suggests non-constant-time comparison: all_wrong={:?}, one_wrong={:?}\",\n            ratio,\n            all_wrong_duration,\n            one_wrong_duration,\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Some(\"my-token\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn extract_bearer_missing_header() {\n        let headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        assert_eq!(extract_bearer(&headers), None);\n    }\n\n    /// Check and increment rate limit counter for search UI access.\n    /// Returns (allowed, remaining_requests, reset_after_seconds).\n    pub fn check_rate_limit_searchui(\n        &self,\n        ip: &str,\n        limit: u64,\n        window_seconds: u64,\n    ) -> Result<(bool, u64, i64)> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let ip = ip.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:searchui:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n\n            // Get current count\n            let current: Option<u64> = conn.get(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            // Get TTL\n            let ttl: i64 = conn.ttl(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let count = current.unwrap_or(0);\n\n            // Check if limit exceeded\n            if count >= limit {\n                return Ok((false, 0, ttl.max(0)));\n            }\n\n            // Increment counter\n            let new_count: u64 = conn.incr(&key, 1).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            // Set expiry on first request\n            if count == 0 {\n                let _: () = conn.expire(&key, window_seconds as i64).await\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n            }\n\n            Ok((true, limit.saturating_sub(new_count), ttl.max(0)))\n        })\n    }\n\n    // --- Rate limiting: admin_login ---\n\n    /// Check admin login rate limit and exponential backoff.\n    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n    pub fn check_rate_limit_admin_login(\n        &self,\n        ip: &str,\n        limit: u64,\n        window_seconds: u64,\n    ) -> Result<(bool, Option<u64>)> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let ip = ip.to_string();\n        let backoff_key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n        let key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:adminlogin:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = pool.manager.lock().await;\n\n            // Check if we're in backoff mode\n            let backoff_fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&backoff_key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if !backoff_fields.is_empty() {\n                let next_allowed_at = get_field_i64(&backoff_fields, \"next_allowed_at\")?;\n                let now = now_ms();\n                if next_allowed_at > now {\n                    let wait_seconds = ((next_allowed_at - now) / 1000) as u64;\n                    return Ok((false, Some(wait_seconds)));\n                }\n                // Backoff expired, clear it\n                let _: () = conn.del(&backoff_key).await\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n            }\n\n            // Check standard rate limit\n            let current: Option<u64> = conn.get(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let count = current.unwrap_or(0);\n\n            // Check if limit exceeded\n            if count >= limit {\n                return Ok((false, None));\n            }\n\n            // Increment counter\n            let mut pipe = pipe();\n            pipe.incr(&key, 1);\n            pipe.expire(&key, window_seconds as i64);\n            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n\n            Ok((true, None))\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed admin login attempt and return backoff if triggered.\n    /// Returns Some(wait_seconds) if backoff was triggered, None otherwise.\n    pub fn record_failure_admin_login(\n        &self,\n        ip: &str,\n        failed_threshold: u32,\n        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n    ) -> Result<Option<u64>> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let ip = ip.to_string();\n        let backoff_key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = pool.manager.lock().await;\n\n            // Check if already in backoff\n            let backoff_fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&backoff_key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let current_failed: u64 = if backoff_fields.is_empty() {\n                0\n            } else {\n                get_field_i64(&backoff_fields, \"failed_count\")? as u64\n            };\n\n            let new_failed = current_failed + 1;\n\n            // Check if we should enter backoff mode\n            if new_failed >= failed_threshold as u64 {\n                let backoff_exponent = (new_failed.saturating_sub(failed_threshold as u64) as u32).min(7);\n                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << backoff_exponent);\n                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n\n                let now = now_ms();\n                let next_allowed_at = now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000);\n\n                let mut pipe = pipe();\n                pipe.hset(&backoff_key, \"failed_count\", new_failed as i64);\n                pipe.hset(&backoff_key, \"next_allowed_at\", next_allowed_at);\n                pipe.expire(&backoff_key, (backoff_seconds as i64 + 60) as i64);\n                pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n\n                return Ok(Some(backoff_seconds));\n            }\n\n            // Just update the failed count\n            let _: () = conn.hset(&backoff_key, \"failed_count\", new_failed as i64).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            Ok(None)\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Reset admin login rate limit on successful login.\n    pub fn reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&self, ip: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let ip = ip.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:adminlogin:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n        let backoff_key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut pipe = pipe();\n            pipe.del(&key);\n            pipe.del(&backoff_key);\n            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    // --- search_ui_scoped_key ---\n\n    /// Get the current scoped key for an index.\n    pub fn get_search_ui_scoped_key(&self, index_uid: &str) -> Result<Option<SearchUiScopedKey>> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let index_uid = index_uid.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:search_ui_scoped_key:{}\", key_prefix, index_uid);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if fields.is_empty() {\n                Ok(None)\n            } else {\n                Ok(Some(SearchUiScopedKey {\n                    index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                    primary_key: get_field_string(&fields, \"primary_key\")?,\n                    primary_uid: get_field_string(&fields, \"primary_uid\")?,\n P10 Admin session revocation acceptance tests (plan §9).\n//!\n//! Tests the login → logout → revoked-cookie replay flow:\n//! 1. Create admin session in Redis (simulates login)\n//! 2. Revoke session (simulates logout, publishes to Pub/Sub)\n//! 3. Verify session rejected on the originating pod\n//! 4. Verify session rejected on a second pod via Pub/Sub propagation\n//! 5. Verify session lookup in Redis returns revoked=true\n//! 6. Verify non-revoked sessions remain valid\n\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\nuse dashmap::DashMap;\n\nuse miroir_core::task_store::{NewAdminSession, RedisTaskStore, TaskStore};\nuse testcontainers::runners::AsyncRunner;\nuse testcontainers_modules::redis::Redis;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn redis_store() -> (RedisTaskStore, String) {\n    let node = Redis::default();\n    let container = node.start().await.expect(\"start redis\");\n    let port = container\n        .get_host_port_ipv4(6379)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"get port\");\n    let url = format!(\"redis://localhost:{port}\");\n    let store = RedisTaskStore::open(&url).await.expect(\"redis connect\");\n    (store, url)\n}\n\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\nfn make_session(id: &str) -> NewAdminSession {\n    NewAdminSession {\n        session_id: id.to_string(),\n        csrf_token: format!(\"csrf-{id}\"),\n        admin_key_hash: format!(\"hash-{id}\"),\n        created_at: now_ms(),\n        expires_at: now_ms() + 3_600_000, // 1 hour\n        user_agent: Some(\"test-agent\".to_string()),\n        source_ip: Some(\"127.0.0.1\".to_string()),\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Login → logout → replay: session must be rejected after revocation.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_login_logout_replay_rejected() {\n    let (store, _url) = redis_store().await;\n\n    // Step 1: Login — insert admin session\n    let session = make_session(\"sess-login-logout-test\");\n    store\n        .insert_admin_session(&session)\n        .expect(\"insert session\");\n\n    // Verify session is NOT revoked initially\n    let loaded = store\n        .get_admin_session(&session.session_id)\n        .expect(\"get session\")\n        .expect(\"session exists\");\n    assert!(!loaded.revoked, \"new session should not be revoked\");\n\n    // Step 2: Logout — revoke session\n    let revoked = store\n        .revoke_admin_session(&session.session_id)\n        .expect(\"revoke session\");\n    assert!(revoked, \"session should have been revoked\");\n\n    // Step 3: Replay — verify session is now revoked\n    let loaded = store\n        .get_admin_session(&session.session_id)\n        .expect(\"get session\")\n        .expect(\"session exists\");\n    assert!(loaded.revoked, \"revoked session must have revoked=true\");\n}\n\n/// Cross-pod revocation: session revoked on pod A is rejected on pod B\n/// via Pub/Sub propagation within 200ms.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_cross_pod_revocation_via_pubsub() {\n    let (store, redis_url) = redis_store().await;\n\n    // Simulate two pods, each with their own in-memory revocation cache\n    let pod_a_revoked: Arc<DashMap<String, ()>> = Arc::new(DashMap::new());\n    let pod_b_revoked: Arc<DashMap<String, ()>> = Arc::new(DashMap::new());\n\n    let pod_a_clone = pod_a_revoked.clone();\n    let pod_b_clone = pod_b_revoked.clone();\n\n    let redis_url_a = redis_url.clone();\n    let redis_url_b = redis_url.clone();\n\n    // Start Pub/Sub subscribers for both \"pods\"\n    let sub_a = tokio::spawn(async move {\n        let _ = RedisTaskStore::subscribe_session_revocations(\n            &redis_url_a,\n            \"miroir\",\n            move |session_id: String| {\n                pod_a_clone.insert(session_id, ());\n            },\n        )\n        .await;\n    });\n\n    let sub_b = tokio::spawn(async move {\n        let _ = RedisTaskStore::subscribe_session_revocations(\n            &redis_url_b,\n            \"miroir\",\n            move |session_id: String| {\n                pod_b_clone.insert(session_id, ());\n            },\n        )\n        .await;\n    });\n\n    // Give subscribers time to connect\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(150)).await;\n\n    // Create session on pod A\n    let session = make_session(\"sess-cross-pod-test\");\n    store\n        .insert_admin_session(&session)\n        .expect(\"insert session\");\n\n    // Verify not revoked on either pod\n    assert!(\n        !pod_a_revoked.contains_key(&session.session_id),\n        \"pod A should not have revoked session before logout\"\n    );\n    assert!(\n        !pod_b_revoked.contains_key(&session.session_id),\n        \"pod B should not have revoked session before logout\"\n    );\n\n    // Revoke on pod A (simulates logout)\n    store\n        .revoke_admin_session(&session.session_id)\n        .expect(\"revoke session\");\n\n    // Wait for Pub/Sub propagation\n    let deadline = Duration::from_millis(500);\n    let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n    loop {\n        let a_has = pod_a_revoked.contains_key(&session.session_id);\n        let b_has = pod_b_revoked.contains_key(&session.session_id);\n\n        if a_has && b_has {\n            break;\n        }\n\n        if start.elapsed() > deadline {\n            panic!(\n                \"Pub/Sub propagation did not complete within {:?}: pod_a={}, pod_b={}\",\n                deadline,\n                a_has,\n                b_has\n            );\n        }\n\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n    }\n\n    let elapsed = start.elapsed();\n    assert!(\n        elapsed < deadline,\n        \"Propagation should be fast, took {:?}\",\n        elapsed\n    );\n\n    // Both pods must now reject the session\n    assert!(\n        pod_a_revoked.contains_key(&session.session_id),\n        \"pod A must reject revoked session\"\n    );\n    assert!(\n        pod_b_revoked.contains_key(&session.session_id),\n        \"pod B must reject revoked session (received via Pub/Sub)\"\n    );\n\n    sub_a.abort();\n    sub_b.abort();\n}\n\n/// Multiple sessions: revoking one does not affect others.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_revocation_is_per_session() {\n    let (store, _url) = redis_store().await;\n\n    let session_a = make_session(\"sess-per-a\");\n    let session_b = make_session(\"sess-per-b\");\n\n    store.insert_admin_session(&session_a).expect(\"insert a\");\n    store.insert_admin_session(&session_b).expect(\"insert b\");\n\n    // Revoke only session A\n    store\n        .revoke_admin_session(&session_a.session_id)\n        .expect(\"revoke a\");\n\n    // Session A is revoked\n    let loaded_a = store\n        .get_admin_session(&session_a.session_id)\n        .expect(\"get a\")\n        .expect(\"a exists\");\n    assert!(loaded_a.revoked, \"session A should be revoked\");\n\n    // Session B is NOT revoked\n    let loaded_b = store\n        .get_admin_session(&session_b.session_id)\n        .expect(\"get b\")\n        .expect(\"b exists\");\n    assert!(!loaded_b.revoked, \"session B should not be affected\");\n}\n\n/// Revoking a non-existent session returns false but does not error.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_revoke_nonexistent_session() {\n    let (store, _url) = redis_store().await;\n\n    let result = store\n        .revoke_admin_session(\"sess-does-not-exist\")\n        .expect(\"revoke should not error\");\n    assert!(!result, \"revoking nonexistent session should return false\");\n}\n\n/// Expired session is invalid regardless of revocation status.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_expired_session_is_invalid() {\n    let (store, _url) = redis_store().await;\n\n    let mut session = make_session(\"sess-expired\");\n    session.expires_at = now_ms() - 1000; // expired 1 second ago\n\n    store.insert_admin_session(&session).expect(\"insert expired\");\n\n    let loaded = store\n        .get_admin_session(&session.session_id)\n        .expect(\"get expired\")\n        .expect(\"exists\");\n\n    // Session exists but is expired\n    let now = now_ms();\n    assert!(\n        loaded.expires_at < now,\n        \"session should be expired: expires_at={}, now={}\",\n        loaded.expires_at,\n        now\n    );\n}\n\n/// CSRF token rotation on session refresh does not affect revocation.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_csrf_refresh_preserves_revocation() {\n    let (store, _url) = redis_store().await;\n\n    let session = make_session(\"sess-csrf-refresh\");\n    store.insert_admin_session(&session).expect(\"insert\");\n\n    // Refresh CSRF token (simulates GET /_miroir/admin/session)\n    let refreshed = NewAdminSession {\n        session_id: session.session_id.clone(),\n        csrf_token: \"new-csrf-token\".to_string(),\n        admin_key_hash: session.admin_key_hash.clone(),\n        created_at: session.created_at,\n        expires_at: session.expires_at,\n        user_agent: session.user_agent.clone(),\n        source_ip: session.source_ip.clone(),\n    };\n    store.insert_admin_session(&refreshed).expect(\"refresh\");\n\n    // Revoke after refresh\n    store\n        .revoke_admin_session(&session.session_id)\n        .expect(\"revoke\");\n\n    let loaded = store\n        .get_admin_session(&session.session_id)\n        .expect(\"get\")\n        .expect(\"exists\");\n\n    assert!(loaded.revoked, \"session must be revoked after logout\");\n    assert_eq!(\n        loaded.csrf_token, \"new-csrf-token\",\n        \"CSRF token should reflect last refresh\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Pub/Sub delivers multiple revocations in order.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_pubsub_multiple_revocations() {\n    let (store, redis_url) = redis_store().await;\n\n    let received: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Vec<String>>> =\n        Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(Vec::new()));\n    let received_clone = received.clone();\n\n    let sub = tokio::spawn(async move {\n        let _ = RedisTaskStore::subscribe_session_revocations(\n            &redis_url,\n            \"miroir\",\n            move |session_id: String| {\n                received_clone.lock().unwrap().push(session_id);\n            },\n        )\n        .await;\n    });\n\n    // Give subscriber time to connect\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(150)).await;\n\n    // Create and revoke 3 sessions\n    for i in 0..3 {\n        let session = make_session(&format!(\"sess-multi-{i}\"));\n        store.insert_admin_session(&session).expect(\"insert\");\n        store\n            .revoke_admin_session(&session.session_id)\n            .expect(\"revoke\");\n    }\n\n    // Wait for all 3 revocations\n    let deadline = Duration::from_millis(500);\n    let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n    loop {\n        let count = received.lock().unwrap().len();\n        if count >= 3 {\n            break;\n        }\n        if start.elapsed() > deadline {\n            panic!(\n                \"Expected 3 revocations, got {} after {:?}\",\n                count,\n                start.elapsed()\n            );\n        }\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n    }\n\n    let ids = P10 Admin session revocation acceptance tests (plan §9).\n//!\n//! Tests the login → logout → revoked-cookie replay flow:\n//! 1. Create admin session in Redis (simulates login)\n//! 2. Revoke session (simulates logout, publishes to Pub/Sub)\n//! 3. Verify session rejected on the originating pod\n//! 4. Verify session rejected on a second pod via Pub/Sub propagation\n//! 5. Verify session lookup in Redis returns revoked=true\n//! 6. Verify non-revoked sessions remain valid\n\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\nuse dashmap::DashMap;\n\nuse miroir_core::task_store::{NewAdminSession, RedisTaskStore, TaskStore};\nuse testcontainers::runners::AsyncRunner;\nuse testcontainers_modules::redis::Redis;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn redis_store() -> (RedisTaskStore, String) {\n    let node = Redis::default();\n    let container = node.start().await.expect(\"start redis\");\n    let port = container\n        .get_host_port_ipv4(6379)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"get port\");\n    let url = format!(\"redis://localhost:{port}\");\n    let store = RedisTaskStore::open(&url).await.expect(\"redis connect\");\n    (store, url)\n}\n\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\nfn make_session(id: &str) -> NewAdminSession {\n    NewAdminSession {\n        session_id: id.to_string(),\n        csrf_token: format!(\"csrf-{id}\"),\n        admin_key_hash: format!(\"hash-{id}\"),\n        created_at: now_ms(),\n        expires_at: now_ms() + 3_600_000, // 1 hour\n Tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Login → logout → replay: session must be rejected after revocation.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_login_logout_replay_rejected() {\n    let (store, _url) = redis_store().await;\n\n    // Step 1: Login — insert admin session\n    let session = make_session(\"sess-login-logout-test\");\n    store\n        .insert_admin_session(&session)\n        .expect(\"insert session\");\n\n    // Verify session is NOT revoked initially\n    let loaded = store\n        .get_admin_session(&session.session_id)\n        .expect(\"get session\")\n        .expect(\"session exists\");\n    assert!(!loaded.revoked, \"new session should not be revoked\");\n\n    // Step 2: Logout — revoke session\n    let revoked = store\n        .revoke_admin_session(&session.session_id)\n        .expect(\"revoke session\");\n    assert!(revoked, \"session should have been revoked\");\n\n    // Step 3: Replay — verify session is now revoked\n    let loaded = store\n        .get_admin_session(&session.session_id)\n        .expect(\"get session\")\n        .expect(\"session exists\");\n    assert!(loaded.revoked, \"revoked session must have revoked=true\");\n}\n\n/// Cross-pod revocation: session revoked on pod A is rejected on pod B\n/// via Pub/Sub
